description: The actual total duration of the ad break as recorded during playout by the Ericsson MediaFirst automation system. Used for post-log reconciliation, affidavit generation, and makegood assessment when actual duration deviates from planned.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System (Affidavits)

description: The actual wall-clock time at which the ad break commenced during playout, as recorded by the Ericsson MediaFirst automation system. Used for affidavit generation and post-log reconciliation against the scheduled start time.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System (Affidavits)

description: Indicates whether a proof-of-broadcast affidavit has been generated for this ad break in the Wide Orbit traffic system following confirmed playout. Affidavits are required for advertiser billing reconciliation and regulatory compliance.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System (Affidavits); FCC Sponsorship Identification Rules

description: The event identifier assigned by the Ericsson MediaFirst playout automation system to the break trigger event. Used to correlate the scheduled ad break with the actual playout automation log for post-log reconciliation and technical fault analysis.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: The total number of individual advertising avail slots (unit positions) within this ad break pod. Each avail represents a discrete sellable unit (e.g., a 30-second spot position). Used for inventory capacity planning and traffic scheduling.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; IAB Advertising Standards

description: Indicates whether this ad break is subject to geographic blackout restrictions that prevent its broadcast or streaming in specific markets or regions. When true, downstream distribution systems must apply blackout logic per rights and regulatory obligations.
reference: FCC Blackout Rules (47 CFR Part 76); Rightsline Rights and Royalties Management

description: Positional classification of the ad break relative to the surrounding program content. 'pre-roll' = before program content begins; 'mid-roll' = within program content; 'post-roll' = after program content ends; 'interstitial' = between programs in the schedule grid.
reference: IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST); SCTE-35 Digital Program Insertion Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the ad break within the scheduling and playout workflow. 'scheduled' = planned but not yet confirmed; 'confirmed' = traffic-confirmed with sold inventory; 'aired' = playout completed and affidavit-eligible; 'cancelled' = removed from schedule; 'hold' = temporarily suspended pending resolution.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: Classification of the ad break by inventory ownership and commercial purpose. 'local_avail' = inventory sold by local affiliate; 'network_avail' = inventory sold by the network; 'promotional' = house promotional content; 'public_service' = public service announcements; 'emergency' = emergency alert system override. Drives revenue attribution and traffic scheduling rules.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; FCC Public Service Announcement Requirements

description: The calendar date on which this ad break is scheduled to air, expressed in the channel's broadcast day convention (which may differ from the wall-clock date for late-night programming). Used for daily traffic scheduling, affidavit generation, and revenue reporting.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; FCC Affidavit Requirements

description: A code indicating any content category restrictions applicable to advertising within this break (e.g., no alcohol ads during children's programming, no competitor ads). Enforced by the traffic system during ad assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NO_ALCOHOL|NO_GAMBLING|NO_PHARMA|NO_COMPETITOR|CHILDREN_SAFE|NONE — promote to reference product]
reference: FCC Children's Television Act (COPPA); Ofcom Broadcasting Code; MPA Content Rating Standards

description: The timestamp when this ad break record was first created in the scheduling system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404 Data Integrity Requirements; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the rate card CPM and any monetary values associated with this ad break (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations for international broadcast networks.

Indicates whether this ad break is enabled for Dynamic Ad Insertion (DAI), allowing server-side ad replacement for OTT/streaming simulcast audiences. When true, the break is eligible for addressable advertising via DAI platforms such as those integrated with Akamai CDN.
reference: IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST); SCTE-35 Digital Program Insertion Standard; Akamai CDN

description: The human-readable label or descriptor for this ad break as it appears in the Electronic Program Guide (EPG) and scheduling rundown. Used by traffic coordinators and scheduling operators to identify breaks in the EPG interface.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol

description: The target Gross Rating Point (GRP) value for this ad break, representing the expected total audience delivery as a percentage of the total target population. Used for audience guarantee tracking and campaign delivery reporting against Nielsen ratings.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards

description: Indicates whether a makegood (compensatory ad spot) is required for this break due to a failure to air as contracted — for example, due to preemption, technical failure, or underdelivery. Triggers makegood workflow in Wide Orbit.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; IAB Advertising Standards (Makegoods)

description: The maximum allowable duration of the ad break in seconds, representing the hard ceiling for total ad content that may be inserted. Exceeding this value would cause schedule overrun. May differ from planned duration when flex time is permitted.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: The Nielsen audience rating for the program surrounding this ad break, representing the percentage of total TV households tuned in. Used for CPM calculation, audience guarantee assessment, and scatter market pricing.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Audience Measurement Standards

description: The planned total duration of the ad break (ad pod) in seconds as defined in the program schedule. Represents the total sellable commercial time allocated to this break. Standard pod durations are typically 30, 60, 90, 120, or 180 seconds.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: The pod number identifying this ad break within the broadcast day for the channel. Distinct from sequence_number (which is relative to the program); pod_number is the channel-day-level ordinal used in traffic scheduling and affidavit reporting.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST)

description: The reason code explaining why this ad break was preempted or cancelled if it did not air as scheduled. Used for makegood processing, revenue assurance, and operational reporting. 'none' when the break aired as planned.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; FCC Emergency Alert System Rules

description: The published rate card CPM (Cost Per Mille — cost per 1,000 audience impressions) for this ad break, expressed in the local currency. Represents the base pricing for inventory in this break before negotiated discounts. Used for revenue forecasting and yield management.
reference: Salesforce Media Cloud Ad Sales; IAB CPM Pricing Standards

description: The planned wall-clock date and time at which the ad break is scheduled to begin on the linear channel. Expressed in the channel's broadcast timezone. This is the principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for the break.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; SCTE-35 Digital Program Insertion Standard

description: Ordinal position of this ad break within the parent schedule slot or program. Used to order multiple breaks within a single program (e.g., break 1, break 2, break 3) for traffic scheduling and playout automation.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: Indicates whether this ad break is included in the simultaneous multi-platform broadcast (simulcast) of the channel. When false, the break may be suppressed or replaced on simulcast streams (e.g., OTT, vMVPD).
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST)

description: The number of individual avail slots within this break that have been sold and assigned to advertiser spots in the Wide Orbit traffic system. Used with avail_count to compute sellout rate and unsold inventory.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; Salesforce Media Cloud Ad Sales

description: The total duration in seconds of ad inventory within this break that has been sold to advertisers via the Wide Orbit traffic system. Used to calculate unsold (remnant) inventory and sellout rate for revenue assurance reporting.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; Salesforce Media Cloud Ad Sales

description: The timestamp when this ad break record was most recently modified in the scheduling system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404 Data Integrity Requirements; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

business_glossary_term: Wide Orbit Traffic System Break ID
description: The externally-known break identifier assigned by the Wide Orbit traffic and billing system. Used to reconcile ad break inventory between the scheduling domain and the ad sales/traffic fulfillment workflow. This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for the break as known to the traffic system.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Electronic Program Guide (EPG) entry record in the scheduling domain. Serves as the primary key for this Silver Layer data product.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: Foreign key linking to mediaasset.asset_version. Business justification: EPG feeds published to guide aggregators (Gracenote, TiVo, Apple TV) must include version-specific metadata — HDR flag, resolution, language tracks, subtitle availability. The is_hdr and video_resolut
Reference to the broadcast channel on which this EPG entry is scheduled for playout. Links to the channel master record in the scheduling domain.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: EPG displays must show accurate content ratings for viewer guidance, V-chip filtering, and watershed compliance. Regulatory requirement in UK (Ofcom) and US (FCC) for electronic program guides. Denorm

Foreign key linking to content.content_episode. Business justification: EPG entries describe specific episodes; linking to content_episode enables accurate episode-level metadata delivery to EPG aggregators, rights clearance verification per episode, and repeat/rerun trac

Foreign key linking to distribution.delivery_channel. Business justification: EPG entries are published to specific delivery channels (FAST, OTT, MVPD). EPG feed generation, metadata syndication, and blackout enforcement all require knowing which delivery channel an EPG entry t

Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: EPG entries distributed to MVPDs must reference the distribution agreement to enforce blackout region restrictions and rights window display rules. MVPD EPG feed compliance and distribution agreement
Reference to the media content asset in the Digital Asset Management system (Dalet Galaxy) that is being broadcast for this EPG entry.
reference: Dalet Galaxy Media Asset Management and Workflow Orchestration

description: Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: EPG entries are published to OTT platforms for on-screen guide display. Platform-specific EPG feed versioning, metadata formatting requirements, and rights window enforcement per platform require this

Foreign key linking to scheduling.program_schedule. Business justification: An EPG entry is published from a specific program schedule version. Linking epg_entry.program_schedule_id → program_schedule enables EPG publishing workflows to directly associate published guide entr

Foreign key linking to rights.rights_availability_window. Business justification: EPG publication workflow requires real-time validation that content is within its rights availability window before displaying to viewers. Prevents publishing unavailable content and supports automate
Reference to the approved schedule slot from which this EPG entry was derived. Represents the authoritative playout scheduling record in Ericsson MediaFirst.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: Foreign key linking to scheduling.daypart. Business justification: EPG entries require daypart linkage for audience reach estimation, program discovery optimization, and daypart-based content recommendations. Creates new scheduling_daypart_id FK (role-prefixed to avo

Foreign key linking to content.series. Business justification: Series-level EPG reporting (e.g., all episodes of a series airing this week, series completion tracking for VOD catch-up) requires linking EPG entries to content.series. EPG aggregators use series gro

Foreign key linking to partner.syndication_agreement. Business justification: EPG entries for syndicated content must reference the syndication agreement to display correct rights window information, apply territory blackout flags, and enforce run-count compliance in EPG feeds

Foreign key linking to talent.talent_profile. Business justification: EPG metadata includes featured talent names for viewer discovery, search functionality, and promotional purposes. Broadcasters link talent profiles to EPG entries to enable 'find shows with this actor

Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: EPG feed generation and metadata syndication require linking EPG entries to canonical title records for accurate program metadata delivery to aggregators (TiVo, Gracenote). A broadcast domain expert e

Foreign key linking to rights.holdback. Business justification: EPG blackout entries (is_blackout, blackout_region_codes) are driven by holdback records. EPG distribution systems need direct holdback reference for real-time blackout enforcement without traversing

Foreign key linking to content.version. Business justification: EPG feeds must specify which version (language, resolution, HDR format) is broadcasting so downstream platforms display correct technical metadata. Broadcast operations require version-level EPG accur
The number of ad pods (groups of advertisements within a break) scheduled within this program. Used for advertising inventory planning, Wide Orbit traffic scheduling, and revenue forecasting.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST); SCTE-35

description: Comma-separated list of geographic region or market codes where this program is subject to broadcast blackout restrictions. Used by distribution systems to suppress EPG entry delivery in restricted markets. Populated only when is_blackout is True.
reference: Rightsline Rights and Royalties Management; FCC Blackout Rules 47 CFR Part 76

description: The calendar date on which this program is scheduled to air, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for day-level EPG grid display, daypart assignment, and schedule reporting.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: The distribution window or release strategy under which this EPG entry is being broadcast, reflecting the windowing strategy for sequential content release. Determines rights availability and platform eligibility per Rightsline contract terms.
reference: Rightsline Rights and Royalties Management; Motion Picture Association (MPA) Windowing Guidelines

description: The total scheduled duration of the program in seconds, derived from the difference between scheduled start and end times. Used for EPG display, ad pod planning, and playout automation validation.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; SCTE-35

description: The Entertainment Identifier Registry (EIDR) identifier for this content, providing a universal content identifier used across the media supply chain for content tracking, rights management, and EPG data exchange with MVPDs and vMVPDs.
reference: Entertainment Identifier Registry (EIDR) Association; Motion Picture Association (MPA)

description: The current lifecycle status of this EPG entry within the scheduling workflow. 'draft' indicates pending review; 'approved' indicates cleared for distribution; 'published' indicates actively distributed to EPG feeds; 'cancelled' indicates removed from schedule; 'expired' indicates the broadcast window has passed.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: Externally distributed business identifier for this EPG entry, used in feeds delivered to MVPDs, vMVPDs, set-top boxes, and digital platforms for program discovery. Format: EPG-{CHANNEL}-{YYYYMMDD}-{HHMMSS}.
reference: ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol; DVB SI EN 300 468

description: The version number of this EPG entry record as distributed in the EPG feed. Incremented each time the entry is updated and redistributed to MVPDs, vMVPDs, and digital platforms. Enables downstream systems to detect and apply updates.
reference: ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol; DVB SI EN 300 468

description: The sequential episode number within the season for this EPG entry. Used for viewer navigation and content ordering in EPG feeds. Null for non-episodic content.
reference: ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol

description: The title of the specific episode within a series, as displayed in the EPG. Null for non-episodic content such as feature films or standalone specials.
reference: ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol; DVB SI EN 300 468

description: The primary content genre classification for this EPG entry as distributed in the EPG feed (e.g., Drama, Comedy, News, Sports, Documentary, Reality). Used for viewer discovery, content filtering, and audience analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drama|comedy|news|sports|documentary|reality|entertainment|children|film|music — promote to reference product]
reference: DVB SI EN 300 468 Content Descriptor; ATSC A/65

description: An optional secondary genre sub-classification providing more granular content categorization for EPG discovery and audience targeting (e.g., Crime Drama, Political Comedy, Investigative Documentary).

Indicates whether geographic broadcast restrictions (blackout) apply to this EPG entry (True). When True, the program is subject to blackout rules that restrict distribution in specific geographic markets per rights agreements managed in Rightsline.
reference: Rightsline Rights and Royalties Management; FCC Must-Carry and Blackout Rules 47 CFR Part 76

description: Indicates whether closed captioning (CC) is available for this program (True). Distributed in EPG feeds for accessibility compliance and viewer information. Required for FCC closed captioning regulatory compliance.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 79 Closed Captioning; Twenty-First Century Communications and Video Accessibility Act (CVAA)

description: Indicates whether this program is available in High Dynamic Range (HDR) format (True). Distributed in EPG feeds to inform viewers and set-top boxes of enhanced picture quality availability.
reference: ATSC A/341 High Dynamic Range; DVB HDR Specification

description: Indicates whether this EPG entry represents a live broadcast event (True) as opposed to a pre-recorded or time-shifted program (False). Drives playout automation configuration in Ericsson MediaFirst and is distributed in EPG feeds for viewer-facing live indicators.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; ATSC A/65

description: Indicates whether this EPG entry represents a new, first-run episode (True) or a repeat/rerun broadcast (False). Distributed in EPG feeds for viewer-facing 'NEW' indicators and drives audience measurement prioritization.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; ATSC A/65

description: Indicates whether this program is being simultaneously broadcast across multiple platforms or channels (simulcast). When True, the same content is distributed concurrently on linear and digital/OTT platforms.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; ATSC A/65

description: The International Standard Audiovisual Number (ISAN) uniquely identifying this audiovisual work globally. Used for rights management, content identification across platforms, and interoperability with Rightsline and international content registries.
reference: ISO 15706-2 International Standard Audiovisual Number (ISAN); ISAN International Agency

description: The primary audio language of the program, expressed as a three-letter ISO 639-2 language code (e.g., 'eng' for English, 'spa' for Spanish). Used in EPG feeds for language filtering and accessibility compliance.
reference: ISO 639-2 Language Codes; ATSC A/65; DVB SI EN 300 468

description: The date and time when this EPG entry record was most recently modified, including schedule changes, metadata updates, or status transitions. Used for change detection in downstream EPG feed distribution and audit trail.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; Dalet Galaxy Media Asset Management

description: The date and time when this EPG entry was first published and distributed to external EPG feeds (MVPDs, vMVPDs, set-top boxes, digital platforms). Serves as the record audit created timestamp for this data product.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: The end date of the broadcast rights window during which this content is cleared for transmission on this channel. After this date, the content enters a holdback period and cannot be broadcast without rights renewal in Rightsline.
reference: Rightsline Rights and Royalties Management; Motion Picture Association (MPA) Windowing Guidelines

description: The start date of the broadcast rights window during which this content is cleared for transmission on this channel. Derived from Rightsline contract availabilities and used to validate schedule compliance.
reference: Rightsline Rights and Royalties Management; Motion Picture Association (MPA) Windowing Guidelines

description: The precise date and time at which this program is scheduled to end broadcast on the channel. Used to calculate program duration and to sequence adjacent EPG entries in the linear channel grid.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; ATSC A/65

description: The precise date and time at which this program is scheduled to begin broadcast on the channel, expressed in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. This is the principal business event timestamp for the EPG entry and drives all downstream playout automation in Ericsson MediaFirst.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; ATSC A/65

description: The timestamp of the SCTE-35 splice_insert cue-out signal marking the start of an ad break opportunity within this program. Used by Dynamic Ad Insertion (DAI) systems and Wide Orbit traffic systems for ad pod placement and automated ad scheduling.
reference: SCTE-35 Digital Program Insertion Cueing Message Standard; Society of Cable Telecommunications Engineers (SCTE)

description: The season number within the series for this episode. Used for EPG display and content navigation. Null for non-episodic or non-seasonal content.
reference: ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol

description: A detailed program description (typically up to 500 characters) distributed in EPG feeds for display on digital platforms, OTT apps, and web-based program guides. Sourced from Dalet Galaxy content metadata.
reference: ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol; DVB SI EN 300 468

description: A brief program description (typically up to 100 characters) distributed in EPG feeds for display on set-top boxes and digital platforms with limited screen real estate. Sourced from Dalet Galaxy content metadata.
reference: ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol; DVB SI EN 300 468

description: The total cumulative duration in seconds of all scheduled ad breaks within this program. Used for advertising inventory management, compliance with ad load regulations, and Wide Orbit traffic scheduling.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; FCC Commercial Limits in Children's Programming; IAB

description: The video resolution format of this program as distributed in the EPG feed (e.g., SD, HD, FHD, UHD/4K). Used for viewer-facing display in EPG interfaces and for platform compatibility routing.
reference: ATSC A/53 Digital Television Standard; DVB Digital Video Broadcasting Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the program schedule record. One record per channel per broadcast date per schedule version. This is the primary key of the program_schedule data product.

Foreign key linking to partner.acquisition_deal. Business justification: Program schedules airing acquired content must reference the acquisition deal for rights clearance verification, holdback compliance, and run-allowance tracking. Rights management and traffic operatio

Foreign key linking to partner.affiliate_agreement. Business justification: Affiliate station program schedules must reference the affiliate agreement to track must-air programming hours, preemption rights, and network compensation calculations. Affiliate relations and networ

Foreign key linking to mediaasset.asset_collection. Business justification: Syndication and themed programming blocks (e.g., a sports package, holiday marathon) are built from pre-curated asset collections. Scheduling teams assign a collection to a program schedule block, ena

Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Program schedules must comply with license terms including children's programming quotas, must-carry requirements, and public interest obligations. Required for FCC quarterly compliance reports and li

Reference to the broadcast channel for which this schedule is defined. Identifies the linear channel in the channel master (e.g., NBC East, CNN HD). Drives Ericsson MediaFirst playout automation for the specific channel.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Channel Management; ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol

description: Foreign key linking to partner.coproduction_agreement. Business justification: Program schedules airing co-produced content must reference the co-production agreement for credit obligation display, distribution rights allocation verification, and residuals tracking. Co-productio

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Program schedules incur production and transmission costs tracked to cost centers. Schedule cost reconciliation, budget variance analysis, and production accounting require this mapping. Standard broa

Foreign key linking to distribution.delivery_channel. Business justification: Program schedules are published to specific delivery channels (linear, OTT, FAST). EPG syndication, rights enforcement, and ad insertion configuration all require knowing which delivery channel a sche

Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: Program schedules distributed to MVPDs or affiliates must reference the governing distribution agreement for must-carry compliance verification, blackout restriction enforcement, and carriage obligati

Foreign key linking to rights.license_agreement. Business justification: Schedule approval workflows require confirming the license agreement is valid for the scheduled broadcast period, territory, and channel. Rights clearance status on program_schedule is meaningless wit

Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Program schedules are market-specific — must-carry compliance, blackout rules, and retransmission consent obligations vary by DMA. Linking program_schedule to market_coverage enables market-level comp

Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Program schedules are published to OTT platforms for live streaming and on-demand availability. Platform-specific scheduling (staggered release, platform exclusives, EPG publishing) requires this link

Foreign key linking to finance.production_budget. Business justification: Program schedules are executed against approved production budgets; finance controllers reconcile scheduled programming costs against budget lines for variance reporting and period-close. A media-broa

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Program schedules must satisfy named regulatory obligations — FCC 3-hour children's programming rule, political broadcasting equal-time requirements, EAS compliance windows. The fcc_children_programmi

Foreign key linking to partner.syndication_agreement. Business justification: Program schedules carrying syndicated content must reference the governing syndication agreement for rights clearance status, run-count tracking, barter spot reconciliation, and clearance obligation v

Total number of ad pods (groups of ads in a break) planned in the schedule for this channel day. An ad pod is a contiguous block of commercial spots within a program break. Used for inventory planning and SCTE-35 cue marker validation.
reference: SCTE-35 Digital Program Insertion Cueing Message Standard; IAB Digital Video Ad Impression Measurement Guidelines

description: Name or user identifier of the programming operations staff member who approved this schedule version. Required for audit trail and accountability in the schedule approval workflow. Populated when schedule_status transitions to 'approved'.
reference: Internal Schedule Approval Workflow; SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act Internal Controls

description: Timestamp when this schedule version was formally approved by the programming team. Marks the transition from 'draft' to 'approved' status. Used for SLA tracking of schedule readiness lead time before broadcast date.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Scheduling Module; Internal SLA Compliance Standard

description: Timestamp when the as-run log was confirmed and reconciled against the planned schedule. Marks the transition to 'as-run' status. Used for Wide Orbit affidavit generation, Nielsen ratings reconciliation, and revenue assurance.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; Nielsen Media Research Platform

description: Indicates whether any geographic broadcast blackout restriction applies to this schedule. True = one or more programs in this schedule are subject to blackout in specific markets (e.g., sports rights blackout). Triggers downstream blackout enforcement in distribution and CDN systems.
reference: FCC Syndicated Exclusivity and Network Non-Duplication Rules (47 CFR Part 76); Rightsline Rights Management

description: The calendar date for which this program schedule is planned and transmitted. Represents the broadcast day (which may span midnight for overnight programming). Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per enterprise date convention.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Scheduling Module; ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol

description: The broadcast technical standard under which this schedule is transmitted. ATSC = Advanced Television Systems Committee (North America); DVB = Digital Video Broadcasting (Europe/international); ISDB = Integrated Services Digital Broadcasting (Japan/Brazil); NTSC/PAL = legacy analog standards.
reference: ATSC Advanced Television Systems Committee; DVB Project Digital Video Broadcasting; ISO/IEC 13818 MPEG-2

description: The highest content rating advisory applicable to any program in this schedule for the broadcast day. Used for parental control systems, V-chip compliance, and FCC content standards reporting. Ratings follow the TV Parental Guidelines system mandated by the FCC.
reference: FCC V-Chip and TV Ratings Rules (47 CFR Part 15); Motion Picture Association (MPA) Content Ratings; TV Parental Guidelines

description: Timestamp when this program schedule record was first created in the system. Represents the business event time of schedule initiation. Used for audit trail, SLA measurement of schedule build lead time, and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act Internal Controls; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Reference identifier for the Dalet Galaxy media asset management workflow instance associated with this schedule's content preparation and ingest activities. Links the schedule to the MAM workflow for asset readiness validation prior to playout.
reference: Dalet Galaxy Media Asset Management and Workflow Orchestration

description: Primary daypart classification for the schedule's dominant programming block. Daypart is the time segment of the broadcast day used for audience measurement, ad sales, and programming strategy. Values align with Nielsen daypart definitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EARLY_MORNING|MORNING|DAYTIME|EARLY_FRINGE|PRIME_ACCESS|PRIME|LATE_NEWS|LATE_FRINGE|OVERNIGHT — promote to reference product]
reference: Nielsen Media Research Daypart Definitions; IAB Digital Video Ad Impression Measurement Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the schedule has been configured with Emergency Alert System (EAS) interrupt capability for this broadcast date. True = EAS interrupt slots are provisioned in the Ericsson MediaFirst rundown. Required for FCC EAS compliance.
reference: FCC Emergency Alert System Rules (47 CFR Part 11); Federal Communications Commission (FCC)

description: External identifier assigned to this schedule in the Electronic Program Guide (EPG) distribution system. Used by downstream EPG aggregators, set-top box providers, and digital guide services to reference the channel day grid. Aligns with DVB SI/EIT table identifiers.
reference: DVB Project ETSI EN 300 468 Service Information; ATSC A/65 PSIP Standard

description: Indicates whether this schedule meets FCC children's educational and informational (E/I) programming requirements for the broadcast date. True = minimum required hours of qualifying children's programming are scheduled. Required for FCC license renewal compliance reporting.
reference: FCC Children's Television Act (47 CFR Part 73.671); COPPA Children's Online Privacy Protection Act; Federal Communications Commission (FCC)

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this program schedule record. Tracks the last update across any field change, version increment, or status transition. Used for change detection in downstream EPG distribution and playout systems.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Scheduling Module; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Reference identifier for the automation rundown object in Ericsson MediaFirst that corresponds to this program schedule. Used to reconcile the planned schedule with the playout automation system's rundown for failover and as-run log generation.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management

description: Indicates whether this schedule satisfies must-carry obligations for the channel on this broadcast date. Must-carry is the mandatory channel inclusion requirement imposed on cable and satellite MVPDs by the FCC. True = compliant; False = compliance issue requiring resolution.
reference: FCC Must-Carry Rules (47 CFR Part 76, Subpart D); Federal Communications Commission (FCC)

description: Indicates the Ericsson MediaFirst playout failover configuration active for this schedule. 'primary' = normal automated playout; 'backup' = failover to backup playout server; 'manual_override' = operator has taken manual control of playout. Used for SLA and incident reporting.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; Internal SLA Standard

description: Aggregate rights clearance status for all programs in this schedule as verified against Rightsline rights management. 'cleared' = all programs have confirmed broadcast rights for this date and territory; 'pending' = clearance in progress; 'partial' = some programs cleared; 'failed' = one or more rights conflicts identified.
reference: Rightsline Rights and Royalties Management; FCC Syndicated Exclusivity Rules (47 CFR Part 76)

description: The planned end timestamp of the broadcast schedule for this channel day, including timezone offset. Typically 23:59:59 local broadcast time but may extend past midnight for overnight programming blocks. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Scheduling Module; ATSC A/65 PSIP Standard

description: Human-readable label for the schedule, typically combining channel name and broadcast date (e.g., 'CNN-HD 2024-06-15 v2'). Used by scheduling operations staff to identify and reference the schedule in Ericsson MediaFirst and Wide Orbit Traffic systems.

Free-text operational notes entered by scheduling staff regarding special instructions, exceptions, or context for this schedule (e.g., 'Breaking news override applied to 8PM slot', 'Live sports event extends prime block by 30 min'). Not used for automated processing.

Indicates how this schedule was generated. 'manual' = built by scheduling staff; 'automated' = generated by Ericsson MediaFirst automated schedule engine; 'imported' = ingested from an external programming supplier or network feed; 'template' = derived from a recurring schedule template.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; Dalet Galaxy Workflow Orchestration

description: Current rights clearance state for the content asset associated with this rundown item as verified against Rightsline. Prevents playout of uncleared content and supports compliance with windowing and holdback obligations.
reference: Rightsline Rights and Royalties Management; Motion Picture Association (MPA); FCC broadcast licensing

description: Timestamp at which the SCTE-35 splice_insert cue-in signal is triggered to mark the return to programme content after an ad break. Used for DAI reconciliation and ad delivery verification.
reference: SCTE-35 Digital Program Insertion Cueing Message for Cable and Satellite; ATSC Advanced Television Systems Committee

description: Timestamp at which the SCTE-35 splice_insert cue-out signal is triggered to mark the start of an ad break for Dynamic Ad Insertion (DAI) and addressable advertising systems. Populated for commercial_break item types.
reference: SCTE-35 Digital Program Insertion Cueing Message for Cable and Satellite; ATSC Advanced Television Systems Committee

description: Production format classification of the rundown item using broadcast newsroom terminology. Determines the technical playout configuration and graphics overlay requirements in Ericsson MediaFirst. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pkg|vo|vosot|sot|live|standup|anchor_read|graphic_full|lower_third — promote to reference product]
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; Dalet Galaxy Workflow Orchestration

description: Ordinal position of this item within the program rundown, determining the playout order. Used by Ericsson MediaFirst automation to sequence segments during live show production control.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; ATSC Advanced Television Systems Committee

description: Indicates whether this rundown item is simultaneously broadcast across multiple platforms (linear, OTT, streaming). When true, triggers multi-platform distribution workflows and DAI configuration in Akamai CDN.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; Akamai CDN Streaming; DVB Digital Video Broadcasting

description: Reason code explaining why this rundown item was skipped during playout. Populated only when item_status = skipped. Used for post-broadcast editorial review and rights compliance audit.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; Rightsline Rights and Royalties Management; FCC broadcast compliance

description: SMPTE timecode marking the Start of Message (SOM) — the precise in-point of the media asset to be played for this rundown item. Used by Ericsson MediaFirst for frame-accurate playout cueing.
reference: SMPTE ST 12-1 Timecode Standard; Ericsson MediaFirst Playout and Channel Management; Dalet Galaxy MAM

description: Physical or virtual media identifier (tape ID, clip ID, or server ID) for the pre-recorded asset associated with this rundown item in the Dalet Galaxy MAM system. Used for media retrieval and playout server cueing.
reference: Dalet Galaxy Media Asset Management; SMPTE Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this rundown item record. Tracks editorial changes, status transitions, and timing adjustments throughout the show production lifecycle.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act Section 404; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity and Accountability

description: Identifier from the Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing system for commercial spots or ad pod items within this rundown. Populated only for item_type = commercial_break; used for affidavit generation and billing reconciliation.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; IAB Interactive Advertising Bureau

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each demographic segment record in the audience measurement reference master. Primary key for the demographic_segment product. [_canonical_skip_reason: Role classified as REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this is a reference master for standardized audience demographic classification cells; per-role minimums are exempt, but MASTER_RESOURCE minimums are applied given the entity carries rich descriptive and lifecycle attributes beyond a simple code list.]
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Demographic segments with GDPR special categories (gdpr_special_category flag) and children's data (is_children_segment) must reference regulatory obligations governing collection, use, and reporting

The corresponding segment identifier in Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) for digital audience activation. Enables cross-system reconciliation between Nielsen linear audience measurement and AEP digital audience profiles for cross-platform campaign targeting and DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) on OTT/streaming platforms.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform; IAB Digital Measurement Guidelines

description: Upper bound (inclusive) of the age range defining this demographic cell, expressed in years. For example, 49 for the A18-49 cell. A null value indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., Adults 55+). Null for non-age-based segments.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Lower bound (inclusive) of the age range defining this demographic cell, expressed in years. For example, 18 for the A18-49 cell. Null for non-age-based segments such as psychographic or income quintile segments.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Relative CPM (Cost Per Mille) pricing index for this demographic segment compared to the baseline segment (index = 100). Premium segments such as A18-49 or W25-54 carry higher indices reflecting advertiser demand. Used in Salesforce Media Cloud ad sales proposals and upfront/scatter market pricing negotiations. Confidential commercial pricing data.
reference: Salesforce Media Cloud; IAB Digital Advertising Standards; Wide Orbit Billing System

description: The timestamp when this demographic segment record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Provides audit trail for data governance and lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse environment.
reference: SOX Compliance; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity and accountability)

description: Comma-separated list of broadcast dayparts (e.g., 'Prime Time, Late Fringe, Daytime') for which this demographic segment has primary relevance in programming and ad sales strategy. Informs Ericsson MediaFirst scheduling decisions and Wide Orbit daypart-based ad pod targeting. Null if the segment applies across all dayparts.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; ATSC Broadcast Standards; Wide Orbit Traffic System

description: The date from which this demographic segment definition is valid and available for use in audience measurement, ad sales targeting, and programming decisions. Supports temporal versioning of segment definitions as Nielsen and IAB standards evolve.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: The date after which this demographic segment definition is no longer valid for new audience measurement or ad targeting use. Null for currently active segments with no planned expiry. Enables historical ratings data to reference the correct segment definition vintage.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Standardized ethnicity classification label for ethnicity-based demographic segments (e.g., 'Hispanic/Latino', 'African American', 'Asian American', 'Non-Hispanic White'). Populated only for ethnicity segment types. Aligned with Nielsen multicultural audience measurement standards and used in targeted content programming and ad sales. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hispanic_latino|african_american|asian_american|non_hispanic_white|native_american|multiracial|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Nielsen Multicultural Audience Measurement; U.S. Census Bureau Ethnicity Classifications; GDPR Article 9 (special category data)

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Special Category Indicator
description: Indicates whether this demographic segment is based on GDPR Article 9 special category data (e.g., ethnicity, health status). When true, enhanced consent and data processing controls are required for EU audience data associated with this segment. Relevant for pan-European distribution platforms and Ofcom-regulated services.
reference: GDPR Article 9; Ofcom Broadcasting Code; European Audiovisual Observatory

description: Gender dimension of the demographic cell. 'All' denotes combined-gender segments (e.g., Adults 18-49, Persons 2+). Aligns with Nielsen standard gender breakdowns used in GRP/TRP calculations and ad sales targeting.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: The geographic level at which this demographic segment definition is applied. National segments are used for network-level ratings and upfront sales; DMA (Designated Market Area) segments apply to local market measurement; local applies to individual station markets; international for global distribution platforms.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen DMA Methodology; FCC Broadcast Licensing

description: Weighting factor applied to this demographic segment when calculating Gross Rating Points (GRP) and Target Rating Points (TRP) in audience measurement reports. Reflects the relative population size and measurement panel representation of the segment. Sourced from the Nielsen Media Research Platform and used in Wide Orbit ad scheduling and campaign delivery calculations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen GRP/TRP Methodology

description: Classification of household structure for household-based demographic segments. Used in HUT (Homes Using Television) and PUT (Persons Using Television) audience measurement reporting and in family-oriented programming and advertising targeting strategies.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: The IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0 standardized code corresponding to this demographic segment. Enables cross-platform audience targeting alignment between linear broadcast, OTT/AVOD/SVOD digital campaigns, and programmatic advertising systems. Used in Salesforce Media Cloud ad proposals and Adobe Experience Platform segment mapping.
reference: IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0; IAB Tech Lab

description: Household income quintile ranking (1=lowest 20%, 5=highest 20%) for income-based demographic segments. Populated only for income quintile segment types. Used in premium CPM pricing tiers and upfront ad sales targeting for high-value audience packages.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Indicates whether this demographic segment includes individuals under 13 years of age (e.g., K2-11, K2-5). Triggers COPPA compliance controls on data collection, ad targeting restrictions, and content rating enforcement. Critical for regulatory compliance in digital and broadcast advertising.
reference: COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act); FCC Children's Television Act; Nielsen Kids Audience Measurement

description: Flags whether this segment is designated as a key demographic (key demo) for the organization's primary ad sales and programming strategy. Key demos (typically A18-49, W25-54) command premium CPM rates and are the primary currency in upfront and scatter market negotiations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Salesforce Media Cloud Ad Sales

description: Indicates whether this demographic segment corresponds to an official Nielsen standard demographic cell (e.g., A18-49, W25-54). True for Nielsen-defined cells used in GRP/TRP/HUT/PUT ratings calculations; False for custom or proprietary segments defined internally or via Adobe Experience Platform.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Standard Demographic Definitions

description: The audience measurement methodology used to quantify this demographic segment. Panel-based measurement uses Nielsen People Meter panels; census-based uses set-top box or streaming data; hybrid combines both; modeled uses statistical projection. Determines data quality, confidence intervals, and applicability for regulatory reporting.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; MRC (Media Rating Council) Accreditation Standards

description: The exact Nielsen Media Research standard cell label for this demographic segment (e.g., 'A18-49', 'W25-54', 'M18-34', 'K2-11', 'P2+'). Used as the authoritative cross-reference when ingesting Nielsen ratings data and reconciling audience measurement reports from the Nielsen Media Research Platform.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Standard Demographic Definitions

description: The native segment identifier assigned by the Nielsen Media Research Platform for this demographic cell. Used for direct API integration with Nielsen ratings data feeds, ensuring accurate mapping of audience measurement data to internal segment definitions.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Data Integration API

description: Indicates the distribution platforms for which this demographic segment definition is applicable. Linear segments apply to traditional broadcast/cable measurement; digital applies to OTT/streaming (SVOD, AVOD, FAST); cross-platform spans both; all applies universally. Drives segment availability in Wide Orbit (linear) vs. Adobe Experience Platform (digital) targeting.
reference: Nielsen Total Audience Measurement; IAB Digital Measurement Guidelines; MRC Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards

description: Descriptive label for psychographic segment classification (e.g., 'Early Adopters', 'Value Seekers', 'Sports Enthusiasts', 'News Junkies'). Populated only for psychographic segment types. Derived from Adobe Experience Platform audience profiling and used in premium ad sales targeting and programming strategy.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Taxonomy; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Estimated maximum reach potential expressed as a percentage of the segment universe achievable across the organization's full distribution footprint. Used in media planning to set realistic reach targets for ad campaigns and programming strategies. Sourced from Nielsen reach and frequency models.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Reach and Frequency Methodology

description: Indicates whether this demographic segment is subject to regulatory restrictions on data collection, targeting, or advertising. True for segments including minors (COPPA), sensitive demographic categories under GDPR Article 9, or segments subject to FCC advertising restrictions. Triggers compliance workflow in Adobe Experience Platform and Wide Orbit.
reference: COPPA; GDPR Article 9; FCC Regulations; CCPA

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the demographic segment, aligned with Nielsen and IAB standard cell codes (e.g., A18-49, W25-54, M18-34, K2-11, P2+). Used as the business-facing identifier in ad sales proposals, ratings reports, and audience targeting systems.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Full narrative description of the demographic segment's defining characteristics, measurement criteria, and intended use cases. Provides context for business users in ad sales, programming, and analytics teams on how to correctly apply and interpret the segment in audience measurement and targeting workflows.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Human-readable label for the demographic segment (e.g., 'Adults 18-49', 'Women 25-54', 'Men 18-34', 'Kids 2-11'). Used in reporting dashboards, ad sales presentations, and EPG audience targeting configurations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Current lifecycle state of the demographic segment definition. Active segments are available for audience targeting, ad sales proposals, and ratings reporting. Deprecated segments are retained for historical continuity but no longer used in new campaigns or programming decisions.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Classification of the segment by its primary defining dimension. Distinguishes age/gender cells (Nielsen standard cells such as A18-49) from psychographic profiles, income quintiles, ethnicity classifications, behavioral clusters, and household composition segments. Drives which measurement methodology and data source applies.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0; Adobe Experience Platform Segment Taxonomy

description: Identifies the originating operational system of record from which this demographic segment definition was sourced. Nielsen indicates the Nielsen Media Research Platform; adobe_aep indicates Adobe Experience Platform; internal indicates proprietary segment definitions; iab indicates IAB Taxonomy-derived segments. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Adobe Experience Platform; IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0

description: Indicates whether this demographic segment is a priority measurement target during Nielsen sweeps periods (February, May, July, November). Priority segments receive enhanced panel measurement and are the primary basis for affiliate compensation and ad rate card negotiations during sweeps.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Sweeps Methodology

description: Total estimated population count (universe) for this demographic segment in the measurement geography, as defined by Nielsen. Used as the denominator in ratings calculations (rating = audience / universe × 100). Updated periodically from Nielsen universe estimates and used in GRP, TRP, reach, and frequency reporting.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Universe Estimates Methodology

description: The calendar year for which the universe estimate is valid. Nielsen updates universe estimates annually; this field identifies the vintage of the estimate to ensure correct ratings calculations and year-over-year audience trend analysis.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Universe Estimates Methodology

description: The timestamp when this demographic segment record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit compliance in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Compliance; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity and accountability)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the market coverage record representing a Designated Market Area (DMA) or television market geographic unit.
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; FCC Market Designations

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Market coverage definitions derive from broadcast license service contours and DMA assignments. License technical parameters (power, antenna height) determine market boundaries for audience measuremen

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Broadcast markets are served by specific licensed legal entities. FCC broadcast licenses and must-carry obligations are entity-specific, requiring direct link for regulatory filing and compliance repo

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Market coverage areas determine applicable regulatory obligations (must-carry, retransmission consent). DMA and market type drive specific FCC obligations; links market definitions to compliance requi

Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Sales territories are defined by geographic market boundaries (DMA codes). Territory planning, quota allocation, rep assignment, and market-based revenue forecasting all require knowing which market_c

Indicates whether geographic broadcast blackout rules apply to this market for sports or syndication exclusivity purposes. True if blackout restrictions can be enforced in this DMA; False otherwise. Used by rights management (Rightsline) and CDN geo-restriction (Akamai) to enforce territorial exclusivity.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 76.111; Rightsline Rights Management; Sports Blackout Rules

description: Digital broadcast transmission standard applicable to this market. 'ATSC' (Advanced Television Systems Committee) is the US standard; 'DVB' (Digital Video Broadcasting) is the European standard; 'ISDB' is the Japanese/Brazilian standard; 'DTMB' is the Chinese standard. Determines technical playout and transmission requirements.
reference: ATSC Standards; DVB Project Standards; ISO/IEC 13818

description: Estimated percentage of television households in the DMA that subscribe to cable television (MVPD or vMVPD service). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6234 = 62.34%). Used for carriage fee negotiations, distribution planning, and ad sales reach projections.
reference: Nielsen Local TV Audience Estimates; FCC Annual Video Competition Report

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code identifying the country in which this market is located (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'CAN'). Required for international rights management, windowing decisions, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.

Indicates whether the DMA observes Daylight Saving Time (DST). True if DST is observed; False if not (e.g., markets in Arizona). Affects daypart scheduling, playout timing, and EPG accuracy in broadcast automation systems.

Nielsen-assigned three-digit numeric code uniquely identifying the Designated Market Area (DMA). This is the externally-known business identifier used across ad sales, ratings reporting, and FCC filings. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform.
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; FCC 47 CFR Part 76

description: Nielsen-assigned national rank of the DMA by television household universe size, where rank 1 is the largest market (New York). Used for market prioritization in ad sales, upfront negotiations, and carriage fee calculations. Updated annually by Nielsen.
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; Nielsen Local TV Audience Estimates

description: The date from which this market coverage record became effective and the DMA definition was valid. Used for temporal versioning of DMA boundary and universe changes, ensuring historical ratings data is associated with the correct market definition.
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; FCC Market Designations

description: The date on which this market coverage record ceased to be effective, typically due to a DMA boundary redefinition, market merger, or discontinuation. Null for currently active market definitions. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) history tracking.
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; FCC Market Designations

description: FCC-assigned market identifier used for must-carry and retransmission consent compliance filings. Maps the Nielsen DMA to the FCC's designated market definition for broadcast licensing, spectrum regulation, and MVPD carriage obligations. Required for regulatory reporting under FCC 47 CFR Part 76.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 76; FCC Market Designations

description: Well-Known Text (WKT) representation of the DMA's geographic boundary polygon. Used for spatial analysis, blackout rule enforcement, retransmission consent territory mapping, and OTT/streaming geo-restriction via CDN (Akamai). Conforms to OGC WKT standard.
reference: OGC Well-Known Text Standard; FCC 47 CFR Part 76; Nielsen DMA Boundary Definitions

description: Estimated percentage of television households in the DMA that are Hispanic/Latino, as measured by Nielsen. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2341 = 23.41%). Used for Spanish-language content distribution decisions, targeted ad sales, and TRP (Target Rating Point) calculations for Hispanic demographic segments.
reference: Nielsen Hispanic TV Audience Measurement; Nielsen Universe Estimates

description: Nielsen baseline estimate of the percentage of television households in the DMA that are using television during an average primetime daypart. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.5823 = 58.23%). Used as a reference benchmark for HUT-based audience projections and ad sales planning.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen HUT Methodology

description: Official human-readable name of the Designated Market Area (DMA) or television market as defined by Nielsen (e.g., 'New York', 'Los Angeles', 'Chicago'). Used in audience reports, ad sales proposals, and regulatory filings.
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; FCC Market Designations

description: Current lifecycle status of the market coverage record. 'active' indicates the DMA is currently measured and used in audience reporting; 'inactive' indicates it is no longer measured; 'pending' indicates a newly defined market awaiting first measurement; 'discontinued' indicates a market that has been merged or eliminated by Nielsen.

Classification of the market scope. 'local' indicates a single-city DMA; 'regional' indicates a multi-city or multi-state DMA; 'national' indicates a national aggregate market; 'international' indicates a non-US market territory. Used for ad sales segmentation, carriage fee tiers, and programming distribution decisions.
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; FCC Market Designations

description: Frequency at which Nielsen provides audience measurement data for this market. 'continuous' indicates year-round metered measurement (PPM markets); 'sweeps_only' indicates measurement only during sweeps periods (diary markets); 'quarterly' or 'annual' for smaller markets with less frequent measurement cycles.
reference: Nielsen Local TV Measurement; Nielsen PPM and Diary Methodology

description: Nielsen measurement methodology applied to this market. 'PPM' (Portable People Meter) is the electronic passive measurement used in large markets; 'diary' is the paper-based recall method used in smaller markets; 'hybrid' indicates a combination of both. Determines the precision and frequency of ratings data available for this market.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen PPM Methodology; Nielsen Diary Methodology

description: Median annual household income (in USD) for the DMA, sourced from U.S. Census Bureau data as incorporated into Nielsen demographic profiles. Used for audience demographic segmentation, CPM (Cost Per Mille) pricing tiers, and advertiser targeting qualification.
reference: U.S. Census Bureau American Community Survey; Nielsen Demographic Profiles

description: Indicates whether FCC must-carry rules apply to broadcast stations in this market, requiring cable operators (MVPDs) to carry local broadcast signals. True if must-carry obligations exist; False otherwise. Critical for retransmission consent negotiations and MVPD carriage compliance.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 76 Subpart D; Cable Television Consumer Protection and Competition Act of 1992

description: The native market identifier as assigned and used within the Nielsen Media Research Platform source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the Nielsen source system, and audit of universe estimate ingestion.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Dalet Galaxy MAM Integration

description: Total number of television stations measured by Nielsen within this DMA. Indicates the competitive landscape of the local market and is used in share calculations and ad sales competitive analysis.
reference: Nielsen Local TV Measurement; Nielsen Station Index

description: Estimated percentage of television households in the DMA that access video content via OTT (Over-The-Top) streaming services. Expressed as a decimal. Used for digital distribution planning, SVOD/AVOD audience projections, and DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) inventory estimation.
reference: Nielsen Streaming Meter; IAB Digital Video Measurement Guidelines

description: Nielsen-estimated total population (persons 2+) residing within the DMA boundary. Used as the denominator for person-based ratings metrics including PUT (Persons Using Television) and TRP (Target Rating Point) calculations. Updated annually in the Nielsen universe estimates release.
reference: Nielsen Local TV Audience Estimates; Nielsen Universe Estimates; U.S. Census Bureau

description: ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code representing the predominant language spoken in the DMA (e.g., 'en' for English, 'es' for Spanish). Used for content localization decisions, Spanish-language network distribution, and audience demographic targeting.
reference: ISO 639-1; ISO 639-2; Nielsen Hispanic TV Audience Measurement

description: Name of the primary city or metropolitan area that anchors and names the DMA (e.g., 'New York', 'Los Angeles', 'Chicago'). Used in market identification, ad sales materials, and regulatory filings.
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; FCC Market Designations

description: Nielsen baseline estimate of the percentage of persons (2+) in the DMA who are using television during an average primetime daypart. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.4612 = 46.12%). Used as a reference benchmark for PUT-based audience projections and TRP calculations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen PUT Methodology

description: Timestamp when this market coverage record was first created in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with SOX financial reporting controls that require data provenance.
Timestamp when this market coverage record was most recently updated in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.

Primary broadcast regulatory authority governing this market. 'FCC' for US markets; 'Ofcom' for UK markets; 'CRTC' for Canadian markets; 'ACMA' for Australian markets; 'other' for all other jurisdictions. Determines applicable must-carry, retransmission consent, content standards, and licensing requirements.
reference: FCC Broadcast Licensing; Ofcom Broadcasting Code; CRTC Broadcasting Act

description: Indicates whether retransmission consent rules apply to broadcast stations in this market, allowing broadcasters to negotiate compensation from MVPDs for carriage of their signals. True if retransmission consent is applicable; False otherwise.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 76; Cable Television Consumer Protection and Competition Act of 1992

description: ISO 3166-2 two-letter state or province code of the primary state in which the DMA's principal city is located (e.g., 'NY', 'CA', 'IL'). Used for geographic segmentation, blackout rule enforcement, and retransmission consent jurisdiction determination.

Indicates whether this market participates in Nielsen sweeps periods (intensive ratings measurement periods in February, May, July, and November). True if the market is measured during sweeps; False if it is a non-sweeps market. Affects programming scheduling strategy and ad sales pricing.
reference: Nielsen Sweeps Methodology; Nielsen Local TV Measurement

description: IANA time zone identifier for the primary time zone of the DMA (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'America/Los_Angeles'). Critical for daypart scheduling, playout automation in Ericsson MediaFirst, EPG (Electronic Program Guide) alignment, and ad scheduling in Wide Orbit Traffic system.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; ATSC A/65 Program and System Information Protocol

description: Total number of counties or county-equivalents included within the DMA boundary. Nielsen defines DMA boundaries at the county level. Used for geographic coverage analysis, must-carry compliance, and retransmission consent territory verification.
reference: Nielsen DMA County Assignment Files; FCC 47 CFR Part 76

description: Nielsen-estimated total number of television households within the DMA boundary. This is the principal quantitative measurement fact for the market and serves as the denominator for all local ratings calculations, GRP/TRP computations, and CPM-based ad pricing. Updated annually in the Nielsen universe estimates release.
reference: Nielsen Local TV Audience Estimates; Nielsen Universe Estimates

description: The date from which the current Nielsen universe estimates (TV household and population) became effective for this market. Used to track the currency of universe data and ensure ratings calculations use the correct denominator for the applicable measurement period.

The calendar year for which the Nielsen universe estimates (TV household and population figures) are valid. Nielsen releases updated universe estimates annually, typically effective in the fall. Used to version and audit the universe figures used in ratings calculations.
