description: The precise date and time (including timezone offset) at which the program began airing. Used for exact scheduling alignment and daypart classification.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Ericsson MediaFirst Playout

description: The percentage of the program's total audience represented by the primary target demographic (typically A18-49). Indicates how well the program indexes against its target audience and is used in ad sales proposals and programming strategy.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Composition Standards

description: The average number of persons or households tuned to the program during an average minute of the telecast. Expressed in thousands (000s). AA is the primary audience volume metric used in GRP and CPM calculations for ad sales.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Average Audience (AA) Metric

description: The unduplicated number of different persons or households that tuned to the program for at least one minute across all airings within a defined period. Expressed in thousands (000s). Cume measures total reach and is critical for frequency management in ad campaign planning.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Cumulative Audience (Cume) Metric

description: The percentage of Adults aged 18-49 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. A18-49 is the most commercially valuable and widely traded demographic in US television advertising, serving as the primary currency for primetime ad sales.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Adults 18-49 (A18-49) Demographic Standard

description: The percentage of Adults aged 25-54 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. A25-54 is a key secondary demographic used in news programming, cable networks, and certain advertiser categories.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Adults 25-54 (A25-54) Demographic Standard

description: The percentage of Children aged 2-11 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. K2-11 is the primary demographic for children's programming and toy/food advertisers. Subject to COPPA compliance requirements for data handling.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Children 2-11 (K2-11) Demographic Standard; COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act)

description: The percentage of Men aged 18-49 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. M18-49 is a primary demographic for sports, automotive, and technology advertisers.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Men 18-49 (M18-49) Demographic Standard

description: The percentage of Women aged 18-49 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. W18-49 is a primary demographic for lifestyle, beauty, and household product advertisers.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Women 18-49 (W18-49) Demographic Standard

description: The average number of times a unique person or household was exposed to the program within the measurement period. Frequency = GRP / Reach. Used with reach for ad campaign effectiveness analysis and makegoods calculations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Reach and Frequency Standards

description: The sum of all ratings delivered by an advertising schedule or program, representing total audience delivery without deduplication. GRP = Reach × Frequency. A fundamental currency metric in ad sales negotiations, upfront deals, and scatter market transactions.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB GRP Standard; MRC

description: The percentage of all television households in the measured universe that were tuned to this program during the measurement period. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 5.2 = 5.2%). The foundational Nielsen rating metric used for ad pricing and programming evaluation.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; MRC (Media Rating Council) Minimum Standards

description: The percentage of households using television (HUT) that were tuned to this program. Share = (Program Audience / HUT) × 100. Measures competitive performance relative to active TV viewers at the same time.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Media Rating Research

description: The percentage of all television households in the universe that had their television sets in use during the measurement period. HUT is the denominator for household share calculations and a key indicator of overall TV viewing activity.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — HUT (Homes Using Television) Standard

description: The total number of person-level exposures to the program, calculated as average audience multiplied by the number of airings. Expressed in absolute numbers (not thousands). Impressions are the transactional currency for digital and cross-platform ad sales.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Impression Measurement Guidelines; MRC

description: The timestamp at which this Nielsen rating record was ingested into the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer from the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used for data freshness monitoring, SLA tracking, and audit trail.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards; Nielsen Media Research Platform SLA

description: The timestamp of the most recent update to this rating record in the Silver layer, reflecting revisions from Nielsen (e.g., preliminary to final, or restated ratings). Supports change tracking and downstream consumer notification.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

description: Nielsen Media Research Platform's proprietary alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the program title within the Nielsen measurement system. Used as the business identifier for cross-referencing ratings data with Nielsen deliverables.

The date on which Nielsen Media Research officially published or delivered this rating record to the broadcaster. Distinguishes the measurement air date from the data delivery date, which is critical for tracking preliminary vs. final rating release cycles.

The ISO 8601 week identifier (e.g., 2024-W23) of the Nielsen measurement week in which this rating was reported. Nielsen measurement weeks run Monday through Sunday. Used for weekly ratings aggregation and trend analysis.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; ISO 8601 Week Date Standard

description: The percentage of all persons in the measured universe who were watching television during the measurement period. PUT is the denominator for persons share calculations and complements HUT for demographic audience analysis.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — PUT (Persons Using Television) Standard

description: Lifecycle status of this Nielsen rating record indicating whether the data is preliminary (fast nationals), final, or has been revised. Ad sales and programming decisions must distinguish preliminary from final ratings. Preliminary ratings are typically released within 24 hours; final ratings follow within days.

The unduplicated number of unique persons or households exposed to the program at least once within the measurement period. Expressed in thousands (000s). Reach is a core metric for ad campaign planning and audience delivery reporting.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Reach and Frequency Standards

description: The native record identifier assigned by the Nielsen Media Research Platform for this rating record. Preserved in the Silver layer for data lineage, reconciliation, and re-ingestion traceability back to the authoritative source system.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Data Lineage Best Practices

description: Indicates whether this rating was measured during a Nielsen Sweeps period (February, May, July, November intensive measurement periods). Sweeps ratings carry elevated importance for affiliate compensation, ad rate setting, and programming strategy.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Sweeps Measurement Periods

description: The average number of minutes that viewers in the measured audience spent watching the program. A key engagement metric used in programming decisions and content performance analysis. Expressed in minutes.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Time Spent Viewing (TSV) Metric

description: The total number of persons or households that tuned to the program for at least six consecutive minutes. Expressed in thousands (000s). TA provides a broader reach measure than AA and is used for program promotion and scheduling decisions.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Total Audience (TA) Metric

description: Rating points delivered specifically to a defined target demographic audience (e.g., Adults 18-49). TRP = (Target Audience Impressions / Target Universe) × 100. Used by advertisers to evaluate efficiency of reaching their specific target demographic.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — TRP (Target Rating Point) Standard; IAB

description: The total estimated number of television households in the measured market or national universe used as the denominator for household rating calculations. Expressed in thousands (000s). Nielsen updates universe estimates annually based on census data.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Universe Estimates; U.S. Census Bureau

description: The total estimated number of persons in the measured market or national universe used as the denominator for persons rating calculations. Expressed in thousands (000s). Segmented by demographic group for TRP calculations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Universe Estimates; U.S. Census Bureau

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the audience measurement panel record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the audience_panel entity.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Management

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Panel recruitment and weighting tied to licensed station coverage areas. Nielsen panels structured around broadcast license service contours to ensure representative sampling for ratings currency.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Audience measurement panels incur operational costs (panelist recruitment, maintenance, data processing, quality control) that must be allocated to research and insights cost centers for budget tracki

Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: A measurement panel is designed to represent a specific demographic composition and is associated with a primary demographic segment for reporting purposes. Adding demographic_segment_id to panel link

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Panels are operated by specific legal entities for regulatory compliance. MRC accreditation and data privacy obligations are entity-specific, requiring direct link for regulatory reporting and audit.

Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Audience panels are defined for specific DMAs/markets. The dma_code and dma_name are redundant with market_coverage master data. Normalizing ensures consistency with market definitions used across nie

Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Nielsen streaming measurement panels are configured per OTT platform. The streaming_measurement_flag on panel indicates OTT measurement capability. Audience measurement operations require panel-to-pla

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Measurement panels must comply with MRC accreditation standards, sampling regulations, and privacy laws. Panel tracks mrc_accreditation_status and consent flags requiring linkage to underlying regulat

Identifier linking this audience panel definition to a corresponding audience segment or profile group in Adobe Experience Platform (AEP). Enables cross-platform audience activation by mapping Nielsen panel demographics to digital audience segments for personalization and targeted ad delivery.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Audience Segmentation; IAB Digital Audience Standards

description: Percentage of recruited panel households or individuals who are actively and consistently providing usable measurement data. A declining cooperation rate is an early indicator of panel quality degradation and may trigger MRC review. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8500 = 85%).
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Audience Measurement; Nielsen Panel Quality Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code indicating the primary country of coverage for this panel (e.g., USA, GBR, AUS). Required for international panels and regulatory reporting to Ofcom and the European Audiovisual Observatory.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; Ofcom Broadcasting Standards; European Audiovisual Observatory

description: Timestamp when this audience panel record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and compliance with SOX financial reporting controls that require traceability of audience data used in revenue recognition.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 8601

description: Technical mechanism used to collect viewing data from panel members. Electronic meters (people meters) require active button-pressing; set-top-box (STB) and smart TV ACR (Automatic Content Recognition) are passive; diary is self-reported. Determines the granularity and reliability of minute-by-minute audience data.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Data Collection Methodology; ATSC Standards; MRC Accreditation Standards

description: Narrative or structured description of the demographic breakdowns captured by this panel (e.g., 'Age 2+, Adults 18-49, Adults 25-54, Persons 2-17, Households by income quintile'). Defines which demographic cuts are available for TRP (Target Rating Point) calculations and demographic audience guarantees in ad sales.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Demographic Reporting; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Date on which this panel was retired or superseded by a newer panel vintage. Null for currently active panels. Used to scope historical ratings queries to the correct panel vintage and to manage panel transitions during sweeps periods.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Lifecycle; MRC Accreditation Standards

description: Date on which this panel became the active currency measurement source for audience projections. Marks the beginning of the period for which this panel's data is used in ratings reports, ad sales guarantees, and GRP/TRP delivery calculations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Activation; MRC Accreditation Standards

description: Breadth of geographic coverage represented by this panel. National panels project to the total US population; DMA (Designated Market Area) panels project to a specific local television market; International panels cover non-US territories.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Geographic Coverage Standards; FCC Market Definition Rules

description: Indicates whether this panel is designated as the official currency measurement source for audience guarantees in advertising transactions. True = this panel's data is used as the contractual basis for GRP/TRP delivery and makegood calculations in upfront and scatter market deals. False = supplemental or research-only panel.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Currency Designation; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The lowest audience rating value (expressed as a decimal fraction of the universe) that this panel can reliably report without exceeding acceptable statistical error thresholds. Ratings below this threshold are suppressed or flagged as unreliable. Typically expressed as a percentage (e.g., 0.0010 = 0.1%). Governed by MRC minimum cell size requirements.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Audience Measurement; Nielsen Reporting Thresholds

description: Date on which the MRC most recently granted or renewed accreditation for this panel. Used to track accreditation currency and schedule renewal audits. Panels must maintain continuous accreditation to support upfront and scatter market ad sales guarantees.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Audience Measurement

business_glossary_term: Media Rating Council (MRC) Accreditation Expiry Date
description: Date on which the current MRC accreditation for this panel expires and must be renewed. Expiry triggers a mandatory re-audit process. Ad sales teams must be notified of impending expiry to manage risk on guaranteed audience delivery commitments.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Audience Measurement

description: Current accreditation status granted by the Media Rating Council (MRC) for this panel. MRC accreditation is the industry standard for currency-grade audience measurement. Accredited panels are required for guaranteed ad buys and upfront sales commitments. Non-accredited panels cannot be used as primary currency.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Audience Measurement; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Nielsen's proprietary internal identifier for this panel as maintained in the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used for direct API integration, data feed reconciliation, and cross-referencing Nielsen ratings reports. Distinct from the internal audience_panel_id surrogate key.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Registry API

description: Free-text field for capturing operational notes, known data quality issues, methodology changes, or special considerations associated with this panel (e.g., 'Sample boosted in Q3 2023 for Hispanic demographic', 'Methodology change effective Jan 2024 — includes streaming'). Used by audience analytics and ad sales teams.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Documentation

description: Indicates whether this panel captures out-of-home (OOH) viewing, such as viewing in bars, hotels, airports, and other public venues. OOH measurement is captured via Portable People Meter (PPM) technology. OOH-inclusive panels produce higher total audience estimates than in-home-only panels.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Out-of-Home Measurement Methodology; MRC OOH Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned by the measurement provider (e.g., Nielsen) to uniquely identify this panel within their system. Used for cross-system reconciliation with the Nielsen Media Research Platform.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Registry

description: Human-readable name of the audience measurement panel (e.g., 'Nielsen National People Meter 2024', 'Nielsen Local People Meter — New York DMA'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and ad sales presentations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Registry

description: Current lifecycle state of the audience panel. Active panels are used for currency-grade audience projections. Suspended panels are temporarily halted due to quality or compliance issues. Retired panels are decommissioned and replaced by newer vintages.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Lifecycle Management; MRC Accreditation Standards

description: Classification of the audience measurement methodology used by this panel. National People Meter (NPM) and Local People Meter (LPM) are electronic metered panels; Portable People Meter (PPM) captures out-of-home exposure; Diary is self-reported; Return Path Data (RPD) is set-top-box passback data; Hybrid combines multiple methods.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Methodology Standards; MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Audience Measurement

description: Statistical sampling approach used to recruit panel members. Probability-based sampling (address-based or random-digit-dial) is required for MRC-accredited currency panels. Opt-in panels are used for supplemental or proprietary measurement. Affects the projectability and accreditation status of the panel.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Audience Measurement; Nielsen Methodology Documentation

description: Cadence at which panel membership is rotated or refreshed to prevent panel conditioning effects and maintain demographic representativeness. Annual and biennial refreshes are standard for people meter panels; continuous recruitment is used for large-scale return path data panels.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Rotation Policy; MRC Accreditation Standards

description: Finest time interval at which this panel can produce reliable audience estimates. Minute-by-minute granularity is available from electronic people meter panels and supports DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) optimization. Quarter-hour is standard for diary panels. Determines compatibility with EPG-based program-level ratings reporting.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Reporting Specifications; SCTE Standards

description: Estimated margin of error (as a percentage) for audience projections derived from this panel at a 95% confidence interval. Smaller panels have higher sampling error. Used by ad sales teams to communicate measurement uncertainty to advertisers and to set makegood thresholds for audience delivery shortfalls.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Audience Measurement; Nielsen Methodology Documentation

description: Total number of sample households actively participating in this panel at any given time. Drives the statistical reliability and margin of error of projected audience estimates. A larger panel size yields lower sampling error for low-rated programs.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Sample Design; MRC Minimum Standards for Electronic Audience Measurement

description: Total number of individual persons (panelists) actively measured within this panel. Distinct from household count because multiple individuals per household are measured for demographic breakdowns used in Target Rating Point (TRP) calculations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Sample Design; MRC Minimum Standards

description: Indicates whether this panel captures streaming and OTT (Over-The-Top) viewing in addition to linear broadcast. Streaming-enabled panels support cross-platform audience measurement combining linear TV, SVOD, AVOD, and FAST channel viewing for total audience reporting.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Cross-Platform Measurement; IAB Digital Measurement Guidelines

description: Indicates whether this panel is used during Nielsen Sweeps periods (February, May, July, November) for local market ratings measurement. Sweeps data drives local ad rates and program scheduling decisions. Only MRC-accredited panels with sufficient local market sample sizes are sweeps-eligible.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Sweeps Methodology; FCC Local Market Rules

description: Total projected population size (in persons or households) that this panel is designed to represent. The universe estimate is the denominator used to convert raw panel viewing counts into ratings percentages (e.g., HUT, PUT, Share). Updated periodically based on census data.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Universe Estimates; US Census Bureau; MRC Accreditation Standards

description: Specifies whether the universe_estimate represents total households, total persons, or TV households (HUT-eligible). TV households are the standard denominator for HUT (Homes Using Television) calculations in broadcast ratings.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Universe Methodology; MRC Standards

description: Timestamp when this audience panel record was most recently modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to detect stale panel configurations and trigger downstream recalculation of audience projections dependent on this panel.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 8601

description: Calendar year in which this panel sample was designed and recruited. Panels are periodically refreshed to maintain demographic representativeness as the population changes. Vintage year is used to track panel age and schedule mandatory refreshes per MRC accreditation requirements.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Panel Refresh Cycle; MRC Accreditation Standards

description: Numeric multiplier applied to this panel's raw viewing data to project sample observations to the total universe estimate. A weighting factor greater than 1.0 indicates the panel under-represents a demographic segment relative to the population. Used in GRP and TRP calculations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Projection Methodology; MRC Standards

description: Description of the statistical weighting approach applied to panel data to correct for demographic imbalances and non-response bias (e.g., 'Iterative Proportional Fitting / Raking', 'Post-stratification weighting by age/sex/race/income'). Critical for ensuring projected audience estimates are representative of the total population.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform — Weighting Methodology; MRC Accreditation Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each reach and frequency report record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.

Foreign key linking to advertising.advertiser. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are generated for specific advertisers to show campaign performance across their portfolio, including cross-campaign reach, frequency distribution, and GRP/TRP delivery. Essent

Foreign key linking to sales.advertising_audience_guarantee. Business justification: Audience guarantees are validated against reach_frequency_reports. Guarantee reconciliation compares contracted reach/frequency/GRP to delivered metrics from reports. Determines makegood obligations a

Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports for broadcast campaigns must reference the station license for regulatory filings (political advertising disclosure, public inspection file entries). Report has is_sweeps_perio

Reference to the advertising campaign for which this reach and frequency report was produced. Links to the campaign master record in Salesforce Media Cloud. Used for post-campaign delivery validation and upfront guarantee reconciliation.
reference: Salesforce Media Cloud; IAB Digital Advertising Standards

description: Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are generated for specific networks/channels to measure campaign delivery by channel. Direct channel link enables automated report generation, channel-specific reach analysis,

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports measure advertising campaign effectiveness by sales team cost center. Sales commission calculations and territory performance analysis require linking campaign delivery metrics

Reference to the daypart (time segment of the broadcast day) for which audience measurement was computed. Links to the daypart reference product. Used to segment reach and frequency metrics by time-of-day for programming and ad sales analysis.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; ATSC Standards

description: Foreign key linking to distribution.delivery_channel. Business justification: R&F reports are generated per delivery channel for campaign delivery analysis and makegood determination. Distribution and ad sales teams require channel-level R&F data to assess delivery variance and

Reference to the demographic segment (e.g., Adults 18-49, Women 25-54) against which reach and frequency metrics are measured. Links to the demographic segment reference product sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Central to Target Rating Point (TRP) calculations and ad sales guarantees.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Reference to the geographic market (DMA, national, or international market) for which this reach and frequency report applies. Links to the market reference product. Used for blackout compliance, scatter market pricing, and geographic audience analysis.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; FCC Broadcast Licensing

description: Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are core deliverables in ad sales proposals and post-campaign analysis. Each R&F report is generated for a specific sales opportunity to demonstrate projected or actual campaig

Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Reach and frequency reports are generated per OTT platform for cross-platform campaign measurement and media planning. The platform_type plain-text column denormalizes ott_platform. Advertisers and ag

Foreign key linking to audience.panel. Business justification: A reach_frequency_report is computed from panel-based measurement data. The report's universe_estimate, reach_persons, and frequency metrics are all derived from a specific panel's universe. Adding pa

Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are often generated for specific distribution or syndication partners to demonstrate audience delivery against contractual obligations. Required for partner performance reporti

Foreign key linking to compliance.political_ad_record. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports for political campaigns require public inspection file linkage per FCC rules. Demonstrates delivery of reasonable access and documents audience delivery for political advertise

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Reach and frequency reports are generated per profit center for business unit performance tracking. Sales organizations require P&L attribution for campaign delivery to measure profit center effective

Foreign key linking to scheduling.program_schedule. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are generated against specific program schedules to validate upfront and scatter deal delivery. Sales operations reconcile R&F reports against program_schedule for makegood iss

Foreign key linking to sales.sales_proposal. Business justification: Sales proposals include projected reach/frequency estimates that must be validated post-campaign against actual reach_frequency_reports. Proposal performance reconciliation compares guaranteed vs deli

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports must track compliance with advertising disclosure obligations, political advertising lowest unit charge rules, and measurement standards. Report has political_ad_record_id and

Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: R&F reports validate revenue stream performance against sold audience guarantees. Finance uses these reports to reconcile revenue recognition with actual audience delivery for revenue assurance and au

Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Reach and frequency reports are generated per program/title as standard post-buy advertiser deliverables. Linking to content.title enables program-level R&F analysis, content performance benchmarking

The percentage of the total viewing audience (persons using television) that was watching the specific network or program during the measurement period. Calculated as the program's audience divided by the total HUT/PUT audience. Used for competitive programming analysis and ad sales positioning.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Audience Measurement Standards

description: The average number of times a unique viewer within the target demographic was exposed to the content or campaign during the measurement period. Calculated as total impressions divided by unduplicated reach. Core metric for evaluating campaign saturation and makegoods calculation.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The cost per one thousand audience impressions for the campaign or programming block, expressed in USD. Used by ad sales for post-campaign delivery validation, scatter market pricing justification, and makegood valuation. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial pricing.
reference: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; Salesforce Media Cloud

description: The cost per one rating point delivered against the target demographic segment, expressed in USD. The primary pricing metric for linear TV advertising transactions. Used for upfront guarantee reconciliation, makegood valuation, and scatter market pricing. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial pricing.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Audit timestamp recording when this reach and frequency report record was first ingested into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage tracking and pipeline monitoring.
reference: Media Broadcasting Internal Data Standards; SOX Compliance

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this report (CPM, CPRP). Defaults to USD for domestic US markets. Required for international market reports and multi-currency financial reconciliation under SOX compliance.

The percentage difference between the guaranteed audience delivery (GRP or TRP) and the actual delivered audience for the campaign flight. A negative value indicates under-delivery triggering makegood obligations; a positive value indicates over-delivery. Used for upfront guarantee reconciliation and scatter market adjustments.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines; Wide Orbit

description: The count of unique persons exposed to the content or campaign at least one time (1+) during the measurement period. Equivalent to reach_persons; included as an explicit effective reach threshold field for consistency with N+ reporting framework used in post-campaign analysis.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The count of unique persons exposed to the content or campaign at least three times (3+) during the measurement period. The industry-standard effective reach threshold used to assess whether sufficient frequency was achieved for advertising message recall. Critical for makegoods determination and campaign effectiveness evaluation.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The count of unique persons exposed to the content or campaign at least N times (as defined by effective_reach_threshold) during the measurement period. Provides a configurable effective reach metric for custom campaign guarantee structures and advanced frequency analysis.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The minimum number of exposures (N) used to define effective reach for this specific report. Configurable per campaign or client agreement (e.g., 3 for standard campaigns, 5 for high-frequency campaigns). Determines the N+ threshold applied in effective reach calculations beyond the standard 1+ and 3+ fields.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The sum of all rating points delivered by the campaign or programming block during the measurement period, representing total audience weight without deduplication. Calculated as reach percentage multiplied by average frequency. The primary currency for linear TV advertising transactions and upfront sales guarantees.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The percentage of all television households in the market that had their television sets in use during the measurement period. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used as a contextual benchmark for evaluating audience share and competitive programming performance.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Audience Measurement Standards

description: Indicates whether this reach and frequency report has identified an audience delivery shortfall that requires a makegood (compensatory ad spot) to be issued to the advertiser. Triggers the makegood workflow in Wide Orbit and Salesforce Media Cloud when set to True.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the measurement period falls within a Nielsen sweeps period (an intensive ratings measurement period typically occurring in February, May, July, and November). Sweeps data carries elevated importance for programming decisions, ad rate setting, and affiliate compensation calculations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Audience Measurement Standards

description: The last date of the measurement window for which reach and frequency metrics are computed. Defines the end boundary of the campaign flight, programming block, or sweeps period being analyzed.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The first date of the measurement window for which reach and frequency metrics are computed. Defines the start boundary of the campaign flight, programming block, or sweeps period being analyzed. Used in conjunction with measurement_period_end_date to bound the audience measurement interval.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Classifies the type of measurement window for which this report was generated. 'campaign_flight' covers a defined ad campaign run; 'programming_block' covers a scheduled content block; 'daypart_window' covers a standard broadcast daypart; 'sweeps_period' covers an intensive Nielsen ratings measurement period; 'custom_window' covers an ad hoc analysis window.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The native report identifier assigned by the Nielsen Media Research Platform for this reach and frequency dataset. Used for source system traceability, data lineage, and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the Nielsen source system.

The percentage of all persons in the target demographic who were watching television during the measurement period. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used alongside HUT to assess total available audience and calculate audience share for programming decisions.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Audience Measurement Standards

description: The percentage of the target demographic universe reached at least once during the measurement period, calculated as (reach_persons / universe_estimate) × 100. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 45.2500 = 45.25%). Used by ad sales for post-campaign delivery reporting and scatter market pricing justification.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The unduplicated count of unique persons within the target demographic segment who were exposed to the content or campaign at least once during the measurement period. The primary reach metric sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used for post-campaign delivery validation and upfront guarantee reconciliation.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The principal real-world event timestamp when this reach and frequency report was generated and delivered by the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Represents the business event time, distinct from audit timestamps. Used to track Nielsen data delivery SLAs and post-campaign reporting timeliness.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Externally-known business identifier for this reach and frequency report, sourced from the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used by ad sales teams to reference specific post-campaign delivery reports in client communications, makegoods negotiations, and upfront guarantee reconciliation.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Current lifecycle state of the reach and frequency report. 'draft' indicates in-progress computation; 'preliminary' indicates initial Nielsen delivery pending validation; 'final' indicates validated and approved for client delivery; 'revised' indicates a corrected reissue; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer version; 'archived' indicates retired from active use.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The version number of this reach and frequency report, incremented each time a revised or corrected report is issued by Nielsen or reprocessed in the lakehouse. Version 1 represents the initial delivery; subsequent versions represent corrections or revisions. Used to ensure ad sales teams are working with the most current data.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Media Broadcasting Internal Data Standards

description: The total number of audience exposures (duplicated) to the content or campaign during the measurement period. Equals reach_persons multiplied by average_frequency. Used as the gross delivery metric for CPM-based advertising transactions and campaign pacing analysis.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Digital Advertising Standards

description: The sum of rating points delivered specifically against the target demographic segment during the measurement period.
