description: Anonymized identifier for the audience member or household profile that generated this engagement event. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform unified profile. Used for audience segmentation and ad targeting without exposing personal identity.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Unified Profile; GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140

description: Ordinal position of the advertisement within its ad pod (group of ads in a break) at the time of the engagement event. Populated only when is_ad_event is True. Used to analyse viewer drop-off rates by pod position and optimise ad pod sequencing for revenue assurance.
reference: IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST); Wide Orbit — Ad Pod Management

description: Version string of the OTT/streaming application that captured the engagement event (e.g., 4.2.1). Used to correlate engagement behaviour with app release cycles and identify version-specific anomalies in audience interaction patterns.

Actual bitrate in kilobits per second of the stream being delivered at the time of the engagement event. Sourced from Akamai CDN performance telemetry. Used alongside stream_quality to assess CDN delivery performance and its impact on audience engagement.
reference: Akamai CDN — Performance Metrics; ISO 23009 MPEG-DASH

description: Indicates whether a geographic broadcast restriction (blackout) was enforced for this viewer at the time of the engagement event (True) or not (False). Supports FCC blackout compliance reporting and rights management audit trails.
reference: FCC — Blackout Rules; Rightsline — Rights Availabilities; Ofcom — Territory Restrictions

description: Total duration of buffering experienced by the viewer immediately preceding or during this engagement event, in milliseconds. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform quality-of-experience telemetry. Used to correlate buffering events with pause and abandonment engagement actions.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Quality of Experience; Akamai CDN — SLA Reporting

description: Total duration of the content asset in milliseconds at the time of the engagement event. Used in conjunction with content_position_ms to compute completion percentage and engagement depth score. Sourced from Dalet Galaxy asset metadata.
reference: Dalet Galaxy — Asset Metadata; Adobe Experience Platform — Media Analytics

description: Primary genre classification of the content asset associated with this engagement event (e.g., Drama, News, Sports, Comedy, Documentary). Sourced from Dalet Galaxy asset metadata. Used for genre-level audience behaviour analysis and programming acquisition decisions.
reference: Dalet Galaxy — Content Metadata; IAB Tech Lab — Content Taxonomy 2.0

description: Playback position within the content asset at the moment the engagement event was triggered, expressed in milliseconds from the start of the content. Core EVENT_PAYLOAD_OR_VALUE for this event log. Enables precise engagement depth analysis, completion rate calculation, and identification of drop-off points.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Media Analytics; IAB Digital Video Measurement Guidelines

description: Motion Picture Association (MPA) or TV Parental Guidelines content rating of the asset at the time of the engagement event. Used for COPPA compliance (children's content restrictions), audience demographic validation, and parental control enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: G|PG|PG-13|R|NC-17|TV-Y|TV-Y7|TV-G|TV-PG|TV-14|TV-MA — promote to reference product]
reference: Motion Picture Association (MPA) — Content Ratings; TV Parental Guidelines; COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act)

description: Viewer's data privacy consent status at the time of the engagement event, governing permissible use of this record for analytics, personalisation, and ad targeting. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform consent management. Mandatory for GDPR Article 6 lawful basis determination and CCPA opt-out compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 6 — Lawful Basis; GDPR Article 7 — Consent; CCPA §1798.120; COPPA

description: Broadcast daypart time segment in which the engagement event occurred, classified per industry-standard daypart definitions. Used for audience measurement reporting, ad sales daypart targeting, and programming scheduling decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|daytime|early_fringe|prime_access|prime|late_news|late_fringe|overnight — promote to reference product]
reference: Nielsen Media Research — Daypart Definitions; IAB — Daypart Standards; Wide Orbit — Traffic Scheduling

description: Operating system of the device that generated the engagement event (e.g., iOS, Android, tvOS, Roku OS, Fire OS, Windows, macOS). Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform device context. Supports technical platform compatibility analysis and audience reach reporting.

DRM (Digital Rights Management) protection scheme active on the content stream at the time of the engagement event. Used for rights compliance verification and to ensure content is consumed within licensed windows and territories. Supports Rightsline rights enforcement reporting.
reference: Rightsline — Rights and Royalties Management; Motion Picture Association (MPA) — Anti-Piracy Standards; ISO/IEC 23009

description: Normalised score (0.00–100.00) representing the depth of audience engagement at the time of the event, calculated by Adobe Experience Platform based on playback position relative to content duration, interaction frequency, and event type weighting. Key metric for non-linear audience behaviour analysis and ad targeting propensity modelling.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Engagement Scoring; IAB Digital Video Measurement Guidelines

description: Processing lifecycle status of the engagement event record in the Silver Layer. 'valid' indicates the event passed all quality checks; 'invalid' indicates a data quality failure; 'duplicate' indicates a repeated event suppressed from analytics; 'quarantined' indicates the event is held pending investigation.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Quality Standards

description: Precise UTC timestamp recording when the engagement action occurred on the viewer's device. Principal EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this event log entity. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for time-series audience behaviour analysis, daypart analytics, and sweeps period reporting.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Experience Event Schema; ISO 8601

description: Categorical discriminator identifying the specific audience interaction captured: play (content start/resume), pause (playback halted), seek (position jump forward), rewind (position jump backward), share (social distribution action), like (positive sentiment signal), comment (text interaction), complete (full content consumption). Core EVENT_TYPE_OR_CHANNEL field for non-linear audience behaviour analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: play|pause|seek|rewind|share|like|comment|complete — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Media Analytics; IAB Digital Video Measurement Guidelines

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the viewer's geographic location at the time of the engagement event, derived from IP geolocation. Used for territorial rights compliance (blackout enforcement, holdback windows), audience reach reporting by geography, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; FCC — Geographic Broadcast Restrictions; Ofcom — Territory Licensing; GDPR Article 3

description: ISO 3166-2 subdivision code (state, province, or region) of the viewer's location at the time of the engagement event. Used for sub-national blackout enforcement, regional audience measurement, and daypart-adjusted analytics.
reference: ISO 3166-2; FCC — Blackout Rules; Nielsen Media Research — Regional Audience Measurement

description: Indicates whether this engagement event occurred during an advertisement (True) or during editorial content (False). Critical for separating content engagement metrics from ad engagement metrics in AVOD and DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) analytics.
reference: IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST); Wide Orbit — Ad Scheduling

description: Indicates whether this engagement event occurred on a stream that was running concurrently with another active stream on the same viewer account (True) or not (False). Used for concurrent stream limit enforcement per subscription terms and HUT (Homes Using Television) measurement.
reference: Zuora — Subscription Entitlements; Nielsen Media Research — HUT Measurement

description: Indicates whether the engagement event occurred during a Nielsen sweeps measurement period (True) or outside of it (False). Sweeps periods are intensive ratings measurement windows critical for ad sales pricing and programming strategy. Enables sweeps vs. non-sweeps audience behaviour comparison.
reference: Nielsen Media Research — Sweeps Period Calendar; FCC — Audience Measurement Standards

description: ISAN (International Standard Audiovisual Number) of the content asset associated with this engagement event. Provides a globally unique, persistent identifier for the audiovisual work independent of platform or distribution channel. Supports rights management, royalty calculation, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 15706 — ISAN; Rightsline — Rights Management; EIDR (Entertainment Identifier Registry)

description: UTC timestamp when the engagement event was received and ingested by Adobe Experience Platform. Distinct from event_timestamp (client-side occurrence time). Used to measure event latency, detect clock skew, and support data quality auditing in the Silver Layer pipeline.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Data Ingestion; ISO 8601

description: Channel or mechanism through which the viewer arrived at the content that triggered this engagement event. Used for audience acquisition attribution, marketing campaign effectiveness analysis, and content discovery optimisation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|search|social|email|push_notification|in_app|external_link — promote to reference product]
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Journey Analytics; IAB — Attribution Standards

description: Playback position in milliseconds from which the viewer initiated a seek or rewind action. Populated only when event_type is 'seek' or 'rewind'. Combined with content_position_ms (seek-to position) to measure skip behaviour and identify content segments that audiences skip or revisit.

Original event identifier assigned by Adobe Experience Platform at the point of capture, before Silver Layer surrogate key assignment. Used for data lineage tracing, deduplication, and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the Adobe Experience Platform source system.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Experience Event Schema; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards

description: Adaptive bitrate (ABR) quality tier of the stream at the time of the engagement event, reflecting the HLS or MPEG-DASH rendition being delivered by the CDN. Used to correlate stream quality with engagement behaviour and identify quality-driven churn signals.
reference: Akamai CDN — ABR Streaming; ATSC A/72; DVB — Adaptive Bitrate Standards; ISO 23009 MPEG-DASH

description: Subscription or access tier of the viewer at the time of the engagement event, reflecting the monetisation model under which content was consumed. Sourced from Zuora subscription billing. Used to segment engagement behaviour by revenue tier for ARPU analysis and churn prediction.
reference: Zuora — Subscription Plans; IAB — SVOD/AVOD/TVOD Definitions

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the audience delivery guarantee record. Primary key for the audience_guarantee data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
Reference to the advertiser (party) to whom the audience delivery guarantee is committed. Sourced from Salesforce Media Cloud account records.
reference: Salesforce Media Cloud CRM; IAB Digital Advertising Standards

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Audience guarantees for broadcast advertising must reference the station license for regulatory compliance (political advertising lowest unit charge, equal opportunities, public file disclosure). Guar
Reference to the advertising campaign for which this audience delivery guarantee is established. Links to the campaign record in Salesforce Media Cloud or Wide Orbit.
reference: Salesforce Media Cloud; IAB Digital Advertising Standards

description: Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Audience guarantees create financial liabilities tracked in specific GL accounts for accrual accounting. Finance requires direct GL account reference for makegood reserve calculations and financial st

Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Guarantee fulfillment must account for content rating restrictions affecting deliverable inventory. Advertisers specify rating-appropriate placements; guarantees calculate delivery against compliant i

Foreign key linking to talent.talent_contract. Business justification: Upfront sales negotiations frequently tie audience guarantees to specific talent participation (e.g., guaranteed GRPs for episodes featuring marquee talent). Sales teams negotiate rate premiums and de

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Makegoods incur costs (production, airtime, inventory) that must be allocated to sales operations cost centers. Finance tracks guarantee fulfillment costs separately from original campaign costs for v

Foreign key linking to distribution.delivery_channel. Business justification: Audience guarantees specify the delivery channel for audience commitment tracking and makegood calculation. Distribution and ad sales operations require channel-level guarantee attribution to reconcil

Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Guarantees target specific standardized demographic segments (e.g., A18-49, W25-54). The target_demographic_code and target_demographic_description are redundant with demographic_segment reference dat

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each guarantee creates GL entries for revenue recognition and makegood liability accrual. Finance links guarantees to GL for audit trail, SOX compliance, and reconciliation of sold vs delivered audien

Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Audience guarantees are often negotiated at the network/channel level for upfront deals. Direct channel link enables automated guarantee reconciliation, makegood issuance by channel, and channel-speci
Reference to the television network or channel on which the guaranteed audience delivery is to be achieved. Links to the network/channel master record used in scheduling and playout systems.
reference: Ericsson MediaFirst Playout; Nielsen Media Research Platform

description: Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Audience guarantees are negotiated per OTT platform as streaming becomes a primary ad delivery vehicle. The platform_type plain-text column denormalizes ott_platform. Ad sales teams sell guaranteed GR

Foreign key linking to audience.panel. Business justification: An audience delivery guarantee is measured and reconciled against a specific Nielsen panel. The guaranteed_grp, guaranteed_trp, and actual_grp_delivered values are all panel-specific metrics — their v

Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Audience guarantees are contractual commitments made to specific partners (advertisers, agencies, networks). Tracking the partner receiving the guarantee is essential for makegood fulfillment and cont

Foreign key linking to production.project. Business justification: Upfront audience guarantees specify which production projects/series will deliver promised ratings. Required for makegood reconciliation, advertiser delivery reports, and sales contract fulfillment. C

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Guarantees are sold by profit centers and impact their P&L through revenue recognition and makegood costs. Finance tracks guarantee performance by profit center for business unit profitability reporti

Foreign key linking to scheduling.program_schedule. Business justification: Audience guarantees are tied to specific program schedules — the schedule defines the inventory against which GRP delivery is measured and reconciled. Guarantee reconciliation and makegood workflows r

Foreign key linking to sales.sales_proposal. Business justification: Audience guarantees are negotiated and documented in sales proposals before being formalized in orders. Proposals specify guarantee terms (GRP/TRP thresholds, makegood provisions) that become contract

Foreign key linking to audience.reach_frequency_report. Business justification: A guarantee is reconciled against a reach_frequency_report that validates whether the committed audience delivery was achieved. The reach_frequency_report contains the actual reach_pct, average_freque

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Guarantees must track compliance with advertising standards, political advertising disclosure rules, and FCC makegood requirements. Guarantee has political_ad_record_id and makegood_trigger_condition

Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Audience guarantees define revenue recognition terms and makegood liability accounting. ASC 606 compliance requires linking performance obligations (guarantees) to revenue streams for proper recogniti
The opportunity identifier in Salesforce Media Cloud CRM linked to this audience guarantee. Enables traceability from the sales pipeline through to audience delivery commitment and reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to scheduling.daypart. Business justification: Upfront guarantees specify daypart-level GRP/TRP commitments (e.g., Prime 18-49 rating guarantee). Direct daypart link enables automated delivery tracking, makegood triggering, and daypart-specific re

Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Audience guarantees (GRP/TRP commitments to advertisers) are made against specific programs/titles. Advertisers buy guarantees tied to named shows; post-campaign reconciliation and makegood issuance r

The actual total GRP delivered against this guarantee as measured by the designated audience measurement source (e.g., Nielsen). Populated during and after the measurement period through reconciliation with actual delivery records. Used to determine whether the guarantee was met and whether makegoods are required.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards

description: The actual total TRP delivered against this guarantee within the target demographic, as measured by the designated audience measurement source. Populated during reconciliation. Used alongside actual_grp_delivered to assess guarantee fulfillment for demographic-specific commitments.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards

description: The timestamp when this audience guarantee was formally approved and activated, transitioning from draft to active status. Represents the binding commitment date for revenue recognition and contractual obligation purposes.
reference: SOX Compliance; SAP S/4HANA Financial Reporting; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing

description: The negotiated cost per rating point (CPRP) for this audience guarantee, expressed in the contract currency. CPRP is the standard pricing metric in broadcast advertising used to calculate the total value of the guarantee and to assess makegood compensation amounts.
reference: IAB Audience Measurement Standards; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing; Media Broadcasting Industry Practice

description: The timestamp when this audience guarantee record was first created in the system. Audit trail field used for data lineage, compliance reporting, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.
reference: SOX Compliance; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); Internal Data Architecture Standard

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the financial values associated with this audience guarantee (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Used in CPRP and total guarantee value calculations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Financial Standards

description: Classification of the advertising deal under which this guarantee was established. Upfront refers to advance advertising sales events; scatter refers to last-minute ad inventory sales; direct, programmatic, and sponsorship represent other deal structures common in media broadcasting.
reference: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; Media Broadcasting Industry Practice

description: The calculated difference between the guaranteed GRP level and the actual GRP delivered (guaranteed_grp minus actual_grp_delivered). A positive value indicates under-delivery and potential makegood obligation. Populated during reconciliation.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing

description: The calendar date on which the audience delivery guarantee measurement period ends. Nullable for open-ended or rolling guarantees. Defines the close of the window during which GRP/TRP delivery is tracked.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards

description: The calendar date on which the audience delivery guarantee measurement period begins. Defines the start of the window during which GRP/TRP delivery is tracked against the committed level.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards

description: Externally-known business identifier for the audience guarantee, used in advertiser-facing documents, contracts, and reconciliation reports. Typically referenced in Wide Orbit sales orders and Salesforce Media Cloud opportunities.
reference: IAB Audience Measurement Standards; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing

description: Current lifecycle state of the audience guarantee. Draft indicates the guarantee is being negotiated; active indicates it is in-flight; at_risk indicates delivery is trending below threshold; breached indicates the guarantee was not met and makegoods are triggered; fulfilled indicates full delivery was achieved; cancelled indicates the guarantee was voided.
reference: IAB Audience Measurement Standards; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing

description: The minimum average number of times a member of the target audience is exposed to the campaign (frequency) committed to the advertiser. Frequency is the average exposures per viewer. Used alongside reach guarantees for campaigns with specific exposure objectives.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards

description: The total Gross Rating Point (GRP) level committed to the advertiser for delivery over the measurement period. GRP is the sum of ratings for all ad spots in the campaign, representing total audience exposure as a percentage of the target universe. This is the contractually obligated delivery level.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards; GRP Industry Standard

description: The minimum number of unique audience members (reach) committed to the advertiser over the measurement period. Reach is the count of distinct individuals exposed to the campaign at least once. Complements GRP/TRP guarantees for campaigns focused on unique audience coverage.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards

description: The total Target Rating Point (TRP) level committed to the advertiser, measured specifically against the defined target demographic segment. TRP is the GRP filtered to the target audience. Used when the guarantee is demographic-specific rather than total audience.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards; TRP Industry Standard

description: Indicates whether the guarantee measurement period falls within a Nielsen sweeps period (intensive ratings measurement period). Sweeps periods (February, May, July, November) carry higher audience measurement scrutiny and may affect guarantee valuation and pricing.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Sweeps Measurement Standards

description: The contractual deadline by which makegood spots must be aired to compensate for any audience delivery shortfall. Critical for revenue assurance and advertiser relationship management.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing; IAB Audience Measurement Standards; Media Broadcasting Industry Practice

description: The total GRP value of makegood (compensatory ad spots) issued to the advertiser to compensate for audience delivery shortfall. Tracked for revenue assurance and advertiser relationship management. Zero if no makegoods were required.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing; IAB Audience Measurement Standards; Media Broadcasting Industry Practice

description: Textual description of the specific conditions under which makegood (compensatory ad spots) obligations are activated. Defines the contractual rules for when and how makegoods must be provided to the advertiser when guaranteed audience delivery is not met. Sourced from the advertising contract terms.
reference: IAB Audience Measurement Standards; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing; Media Broadcasting Industry Practice

description: The external order or tracking identifier assigned by the Nielsen Media Research Platform for the audience measurement order associated with this guarantee. Used to cross-reference Nielsen ratings data and reconciliation reports.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Nielsen Media Research Standards

description: Free-text field for additional business context, special conditions, or operational notes related to this audience guarantee. May include negotiation history, special advertiser requirements, or reconciliation commentary.

Specifies the primary audience measurement metric on which this guarantee is based. GRP (Gross Rating Point) measures total audience exposure; TRP (Target Rating Point) measures exposure within the target demographic; HUT (Homes Using Television) and PUT (Persons Using Television) measure set usage; reach measures unique audience count; frequency measures average exposures per viewer.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards

description: Current status of the reconciliation process comparing guaranteed audience delivery against actual GRP/TRP delivery records. Pending indicates reconciliation has not started; in_progress indicates active comparison; reconciled indicates agreement reached; disputed indicates advertiser has contested the delivery figures; closed indicates the reconciliation cycle is complete.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing; Nielsen Media Research Platform; IAB Audience Measurement Standards

description: The acceptable percentage shortfall below the guaranteed GRP/TRP level before a makegood obligation is triggered. For example, a value of 5.00 means delivery must be within 5% of the guaranteed level. Defined in the advertising contract and used in reconciliation against actual delivery records.
reference: IAB Audience Measurement Standards; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing; Media Broadcasting Industry Practice

description: The total contracted monetary value of this audience guarantee, calculated as guaranteed GRP/TRP multiplied by CPRP. Represents the revenue at risk if the guarantee is not met and is central to revenue assurance and financial reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Financial Reporting; Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing; SOX Compliance

description: The timestamp when this audience guarantee record was last modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data processing in the lakehouse Silver Layer, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Compliance; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); Internal Data Architecture Standard

description: The sales order identifier in the Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing system associated with this audience guarantee. Used for cross-system reconciliation between audience delivery records and ad scheduling/invoicing.
reference: Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing System

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the audience segment record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this master resource entity.

Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: A segment (activation/targeting segment) is built from demographic, behavioral, and psychographic criteria. Adding demographic_segment_id links the activation segment to its Nielsen demographic_segmen

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Audience segments for targeted advertising must track GDPR consent basis, CCPA opt-out applicability, and children's privacy rules. Segment activation_status and dai_eligible flags require compliance

Current lifecycle state of the segment governing whether it is available for use in ad targeting, campaign activation, or content personalization. 'draft' indicates under construction; 'pending_review' awaits compliance or data governance approval; 'active' is live and deployable; 'inactive' is suspended; 'archived' is retired.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Lifecycle; IAB Data Transparency Standards

description: The segment identifier as defined and managed within Adobe Experience Platform (AEP). Used for digital audience activation, personalization journeys, and cross-channel targeting. Enables synchronization between the internal audience_segment master record and the AEP segment definition for OTT and digital platforms.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Builder API; IAB Data Transparency Standards

description: Maximum age (inclusive) of the primary demographic target for this segment, expressed in years. Used with age_range_min to define the Nielsen demographic cell. A null value indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., '55+' segments).
reference: Nielsen Media Research Demographic Standards; IAB Audience Taxonomy

description: Minimum age (inclusive) of the primary demographic target for this segment, expressed in years. Used in conjunction with age_range_max to define the Nielsen demographic cell (e.g., 25 for the 'Adults 25-54' standard Nielsen demo). Null if the segment is not age-bounded.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Demographic Standards; IAB Audience Taxonomy

description: Name or identifier of the data governance or compliance approver who authorized this segment for activation, particularly for segments involving sensitive demographic data, COPPA-restricted audiences, or GDPR-regulated EU audiences. Supports audit trail requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Accountability Principle); COPPA Compliance; Media Broadcasting Data Governance Policy

description: The date and time when this segment received formal data governance or compliance approval for activation. Distinct from created_timestamp — captures the approval lifecycle event. Required for GDPR accountability documentation and internal audit trails. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Accountability Principle); Media Broadcasting Data Governance Policy

description: Primary content genre(s) this segment shows strong viewership affinity toward, used for programming strategy alignment and contextual ad targeting (e.g., 'drama,sports,news'). Sourced from Nielsen viewing behavior data or Adobe AEP content interaction signals. Informs content acquisition decisions and ad pod placement strategy.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Content Affinity Reports; IAB Content Taxonomy

description: The date and time when this audience segment record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage, governance, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Confidentiality); SOX Section 404 (Internal Controls); Media Broadcasting Data Governance Policy

description: Identifies the primary data origin used to construct the segment. 'nielsen' indicates Nielsen Media Research Platform ratings and demographic data; 'adobe_aep' indicates Adobe Experience Platform behavioral profiles; 'first_party' indicates proprietary subscriber or registration data; 'third_party' indicates licensed external data; 'hybrid' indicates a combination of multiple sources.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform; Adobe Experience Platform; IAB Data Transparency Standards

description: Broadcast daypart(s) for which this segment is most relevant or has been validated, expressed as a comma-delimited list of standard daypart labels (e.g., 'primetime,late_night'). Daypart is a standard media-buying time segment of the broadcast day used in ad scheduling and upfront sales. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|daytime|early_fringe|primetime|late_news|late_night|overnight — promote to reference product]
reference: Nielsen Media Research Daypart Definitions; Wide Orbit Traffic System Daypart Standards

description: The rule-based or SQL/JSON expression that defines segment membership criteria as implemented in Adobe AEP or Nielsen. Captures the inclusion/exclusion logic (e.g., age range, viewing frequency thresholds, content genre affinity, platform usage) used to qualify audience members into this segment.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Builder; Nielsen Audience Workbench

description: The date from which this segment definition is considered valid and available for business use. Segments may be pre-built ahead of upfront sales seasons or campaign launches. Enables temporal validity management for segment versioning and historical reporting.
reference: Media Broadcasting Internal Data Governance Standards; IAB Audience Data Lifecycle

description: The date after which this segment definition is no longer valid for new activations. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned expiry. Used to manage seasonal segments (e.g., sweeps-period segments), campaign-specific segments, and segments tied to expiring data licensing agreements.
reference: Media Broadcasting Internal Data Governance Standards; IAB Audience Data Lifecycle

description: Estimated number of unique individuals (persons or households) who qualify for this segment at the time of last refresh. Reach is a core Nielsen and IAB metric used in ad sales proposals, GRP/TRP calculations, and CPM pricing. Distinct from impressions — counts unique audience members, not exposures.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Audience Measurement Standards; IAB Reach and Frequency Measurement Guidelines

description: Unit of measurement for the estimated_reach figure. 'persons' aligns with Nielsen PUT (Persons Using Television) methodology; 'households' aligns with HUT (Homes Using Television); 'devices' applies to digital/OTT platform segments measured by device-level identity.
reference: Nielsen Media Research HUT/PUT Measurement Standards; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Gender composition target for the segment as defined in Nielsen demographic cells and ad sales targeting parameters. 'all' indicates gender-neutral targeting. Used in GRP (Gross Rating Point) and TRP (Target Rating Point) calculations for ad campaign planning.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Demographic Standards; IAB Audience Taxonomy

description: The GRP (Gross Rating Point) index value representing this segment's relative audience weight as a percentage of the total universe. GRP is a fundamental media planning metric equal to Reach × Frequency. Used in upfront and scatter market ad sales to price inventory and measure campaign delivery against this segment.
reference: Nielsen Media Research GRP Methodology; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the segment's reach and rating metrics are calculated on a HUT (Homes Using Television) or PUT (Persons Using Television) basis. HUT is household-level measurement; PUT is person-level. This distinction governs how GRP/TRP values are interpreted and how the segment is applied in Nielsen-based ad sales planning.
reference: Nielsen Media Research HUT/PUT Methodology Standards

description: Household income tier associated with the segment's demographic composition, used in premium ad targeting and CPM (Cost Per Mille) pricing. Expressed as a standardized range label (e.g., '$75K-$100K HHI', '$100K+ HHI'). Sourced from Nielsen MRI-Simmons or first-party subscriber data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: under_25k|25k_50k|50k_75k|75k_100k|100k_150k|150k_plus — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether this segment is approved and technically configured for use in DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) workflows on OTT and streaming platforms. DAI-eligible segments can be activated in real-time ad decisioning systems integrated with Akamai CDN and programmatic ad servers for addressable advertising.
reference: SCTE-130 Dynamic Ad Insertion Standard; IAB VAST/VMAP Standards; Akamai DAI Integration

description: Indicates whether this segment's reach and demographic composition estimates have been validated using Nielsen Sweeps period data (the intensive ratings measurement periods in February, May, July, and November). Sweeps-validated segments carry higher confidence for upfront ad sales commitments and programming decisions.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Sweeps Methodology; FCC Audience Measurement Standards

description: The date and time when the segment membership, reach estimates, and demographic composition were last recalculated from source data. Used to assess data freshness for ad sales proposals and campaign activation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Evaluation Logs; Nielsen Audience Measurement Refresh Standards

description: The date and time when this audience segment record was last updated. Tracks changes to segment definition, status, reach estimates, or compliance flags. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail maintenance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); SOX Section 404; Media Broadcasting Data Governance Policy

description: The corresponding segment or audience cell identifier within the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Enables direct linkage to Nielsen ratings data, GRP/TRP delivery reports, and demographic reach/frequency outputs. Used for reconciliation between internal segment definitions and Nielsen-measured audience delivery.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Platform API; Nielsen Audience Measurement Standards

description: Free-text field for additional business context, caveats, or operational notes about the segment that do not fit structured fields. Examples include data quality warnings, known limitations of the definition logic, special handling instructions for ad sales, or notes on upcoming segment retirement.

The business team or department that owns and is accountable for this segment's definition, quality, and lifecycle management (e.g., 'Ad Sales Research', 'Audience Analytics', 'Programming Strategy', 'Digital Marketing'). Used for data governance, access control, and segment portfolio management.
reference: Media Broadcasting Data Governance Policy; IAB Data Transparency Standards

description: Comma-delimited list of distribution platforms for which this segment is valid and activated (e.g., 'linear,ott,avod,svod,fast'). IANA time zone identifier for the household location. Used for scheduling, program guide display, and live event timing.
Timestamp when this household record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.
Method used to verify the household's identity and location. Multiple methods may be used in combination for higher confidence.
Status of household identity and address verification process. Used to enforce geographic entitlements and prevent fraudulent account creation.
Unique identifier for the parental control configuration record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to subscriber.household. Business justification: Parental controls can be configured at household level (applying to all profiles in the household) or at individual profile level. Currently parental_control has subscriber_id and profile_id FKs. Addi

Reference to the subscriber account that owns this parental control configuration. Links to the subscriber master record.
Reference to the specific viewing profile within the subscriber account to which this parental control applies. Enables per-profile restrictions within a household.
End time of the daily viewing window in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Content access is blocked after this time. Null if viewing_time_restriction_enabled is False.
Start time of the daily viewing window in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Content access is permitted starting at this time. Null if viewing_time_restriction_enabled is False.

reference: Internal Content Taxonomy; Digital Asset Management (DAM) Metadata Standards

business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Opt-Out Flag
description: Indicates whether the subscriber has opted out of data sharing under CCPA. True if opted out, False otherwise. Affects how parental control data may be used for analytics or personalization.
User-defined label for this parental control configuration (e.g., 'Kids Profile', 'Teen Settings'). Helps account holders manage multiple configurations.

Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 language codes (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr') to which content access is restricted. Null if no language restrictions apply. Used to limit content to specific languages for child profiles.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; Internal Content Localization Standards

business_glossary_term: Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) Child Profile Flag
description: Indicates whether this profile is designated as belonging to a child under 13 years of age, triggering COPPA compliance requirements. True if the profile is for a child under 13, False otherwise.
Timestamp when this parental control configuration record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether this parental control configuration restricts viewing to specific registered devices. True if device restrictions are enforced, False if profile can be accessed from any device.
Date when this parental control configuration became or will become active. Supports scheduled activation of restrictions.
Date when this parental control configuration expires or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null if no expiration is set.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Flag
description: Indicates whether the account holder has provided explicit consent for processing parental control data under GDPR. True if consent is granted, False otherwise. Required for EU subscribers.
Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this parental control configuration. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when this parental control configuration was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.

Custom maturity level designation that overrides standard rating thresholds for specific content. Used for granular exceptions (e.g., allowing a specific R-rated film for educational purposes). Null if no override is set.
Maximum number of minutes of content viewing allowed per day for this profile. Null if no daily limit is set. Used to enforce screen time limits for child profiles.
Email address to which parental control alerts and notifications are sent (e.g., when a child attempts to access restricted content). Null if notifications are disabled.

Current lifecycle status of this parental control configuration. Active indicates the configuration is enforced; Inactive indicates it is saved but not enforced; Suspended indicates temporary deactivation by system or admin.
Cryptographic hash of the parental control PIN. Stored as a one-way hash for security. Never stored in plaintext. Used to authenticate access to restricted content or settings changes.
Indicates whether a PIN is required to access content above the rating threshold or to modify parental control settings. True if PIN protection is active, False otherwise.

Indicates whether transactional purchases (TVOD - Transactional Video On Demand) are blocked for this profile. True if purchase lock is active (preventing rentals/purchases), False if purchases are allowed.
Indicates whether search functionality is restricted to only show age-appropriate content. True if search results are filtered by parental controls, False if unrestricted search is allowed.
Indicates whether time-of-day viewing restrictions are enforced for this profile. True if time restrictions are active, False otherwise.
Unique identifier for the payment instrument record. Primary key.

Reference to the subscriber who owns this payment instrument. Links to the subscriber master record.
External identifier for this payment method in the Zuora subscription billing system. Used for system integration and reconciliation.
Type of bank account for direct debit or ACH payment methods. Null for card-based payment methods.
Bank routing number (ABA number in USA) for direct debit transactions. Masked or tokenized per PCI DSS requirements.
First line of the billing address associated with the payment instrument. Used for address verification and fraud prevention.
Second line of the billing address (apartment, suite, unit number). Optional field for additional address details.
City of the billing address associated with the payment instrument.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).
Postal or ZIP code of the billing address. Used for address verification service (AVS) fraud checks.
State or province of the billing address. Format varies by country (e.g., two-letter state code for USA).
The brand of the credit or debit card (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, JCB, Diners Club). Null for non-card payment methods.
Name of the cardholder as it appears on the payment instrument. Required for card-based payment methods.
Timestamp when the payment instrument record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment instrument (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Determines the currency for all transactions using this instrument.
Timestamp when the payment instrument was deactivated or closed. Null for active instruments. Used for lifecycle tracking and compliance reporting.
Reason why the payment instrument was deactivated. Used for churn analysis and payment method lifecycle management.
The month (1-12) when the payment instrument expires. Used for card expiration validation and renewal reminders.
The four-digit year when the payment instrument expires. Used for card expiration validation and renewal reminders.
Cumulative count of failed transaction attempts using this payment instrument. Used for fraud detection and payment method health monitoring.
External reference identifier assigned by the payment gateway for this payment instrument. Used for gateway-specific operations and reconciliation.
Remaining balance on the gift card or prepaid card. Updated after each transaction. Null for non-gift-card payment methods.
Masked gift card or prepaid card code. Full code is tokenized per PCI DSS requirements.
Flag indicating whether this is the default payment instrument for the subscriber. Used for automatic billing and subscription renewals.
Date of the most recent successful transaction using this payment instrument. Used for activity tracking and dormancy detection.
Date when the subscriber accepted the direct debit mandate. Required for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.
Unique identifier for the direct debit mandate or authorization. Required for SEPA and other direct debit payment methods to comply with banking regulations.
Masked representation of the card or account number showing only the last 4 digits (e.g., ****1234). Full number is tokenized and stored securely per PCI DSS requirements.
Name of the payment gateway or processor used for this payment instrument (e.g., Stripe, Braintree, Authorize.Net, Zuora Gateway).
Current lifecycle status of the payment instrument. Active instruments can be charged; expired or suspended instruments require update or replacement.
The type of payment instrument used for subscription billing. Indicates the payment mechanism (credit card, debit card, PayPal, direct debit, bank transfer, gift card, or MVPD billing).
PCI DSS compliant tokenization reference for the actual payment instrument details. Used to process transactions without exposing sensitive cardholder data.
Email address associated with the PayPal account. Used for PayPal payment method identification and processing.
Unique PayPal payer identifier for the linked PayPal account. Used for PayPal billing agreement reference.
Timestamp when the payment instrument record was last modified. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Date when the payment instrument was successfully verified. Used for compliance tracking and fraud prevention.
Status of payment instrument verification process. Indicates whether the payment method has been successfully validated through AVS, CVV check, or micro-deposit verification.

Unique identifier for the subscriber. Primary key for the subscriber master record across all service tiers (SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, bundled). System-generated surrogate key independent of household or device.

Foreign key linking subscriber to their household group. Multiple subscribers may share a household for family plans and concurrent stream management.

Current lifecycle status of the subscriber account. Determines access entitlements and billing state. Active subscribers have full access; suspended accounts are temporarily blocked; cancelled accounts are terminated; trial accounts are in promotional period; churned accounts have completed cancellation.
External identifier linking this subscriber to their unified profile in Adobe Experience Platform. Used for personalization, segmentation, and journey orchestration.
Average monthly revenue generated by this subscriber across all revenue streams (subscription, transactional, advertising). Key metric for subscriber value assessment and segmentation.
Frequency of subscription billing. Monthly, quarterly, and annual are recurring; lifetime is one-time payment; pay-per-view is transactional.
Indicates whether California subscriber has opted out of personal data sale. Required for CCPA compliance.
Date and time when CCPA opt-out was recorded. Required for audit trail and compliance reporting.

Predictive score (0.0000 to 1.0000) indicating likelihood of subscriber cancellation within next billing cycle. Generated by machine learning model based on engagement, payment history, and behavioral signals.
Indicates whether this subscriber account is subject to COPPA restrictions (under 13 years old). When true, data collection and advertising are restricted.
Three-letter ISO country code representing subscriber's primary geographic location. Used for content rights windowing, regional pricing, and regulatory compliance.
System timestamp when this subscriber record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Subscriber's date of birth. Used for age verification, parental controls, COPPA compliance, and demographic segmentation.
Primary email address for subscriber communication, authentication, and personalization. Used as primary contact channel and login credential.
External business identifier for the subscriber, typically used for integration with Zuora billing and Adobe Experience Platform. May be customer-facing account number or external system key.
Subscriber's given name as provided during registration. Used for personalization and customer service interactions.
Indicates whether subscriber has provided explicit consent for data processing under GDPR. Required for EU subscribers.
Date and time when GDPR consent was obtained. Required for audit trail and consent management.
Subscriber's self-identified gender. Used for demographic analytics and content personalization. Optional field respecting subscriber privacy preferences.
Date and time of subscriber's most recent authentication. Used for engagement tracking and churn prediction.
Subscriber's family name as provided during registration. Used for personalization and customer service interactions.
Date and time of subscriber's most recent content viewing session. Used for engagement analysis and retention modeling.
Predicted total revenue this subscriber will generate over their entire relationship with the service. Used for acquisition cost justification and retention prioritization.
Indicates whether subscriber has consented to receive marketing communications. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.
Maximum number of simultaneous video streams allowed for this subscriber based on their service tier. Enforced by playout and CDN systems.
Maximum number of devices that can be registered to this subscriber account. Used for device management and anti-piracy enforcement.

Encrypted PIN code required to access age-restricted content when parental controls are enabled. Stored in hashed format for security.
Primary contact phone number for subscriber support and account recovery. May be used for two-factor authentication.
Subscriber's postal or ZIP code. Used for geographic segmentation, local content recommendations, and blackout enforcement.
Two-letter ISO language code representing subscriber's preferred interface and content language. Used for UI localization and content recommendations.
Channel through which the subscriber originally registered. Used for acquisition analytics and channel effectiveness measurement.
Date and time when the subscriber account was first created. Used for cohort analysis, LTV calculation, and subscriber lifecycle tracking.

Current subscription service tier. SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand) includes basic and premium tiers; AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand) is free with ads; TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand) is pay-per-view; bundle includes multiple services.
Date when the current subscription is scheduled to end or renew. Null for active recurring subscriptions. Used for churn prediction and retention campaigns.
Date when the current subscription tier became effective. Used for billing cycle calculation and tenure analysis.
Cumulative hours of content viewed by this subscriber since registration. Key engagement metric for LTV calculation and content recommendation.
System timestamp when this subscriber record was last modified. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.
External identifier linking this subscriber to their billing account in Zuora subscription management system. Used for payment processing and revenue recognition integration.
Unique identifier for the viewer profile within a subscriber account or household. Primary key.

Identifier of the last device used to access this viewer profile. Used for device tracking, security monitoring, and cross-device analytics. May reference device registration records.

Foreign key linking to content.genre. Business justification: Personalization and recommendation engines require explicit genre preferences. Drives content discovery, homepage curation, and email marketing. Complements behavioral signals with declared preference

Reference to the parent subscriber account that owns this viewer profile. One subscriber may have multiple viewer profiles (e.g., up to 5 profiles per account).

Comma-separated list of Adobe Experience Platform segment IDs that this viewer profile belongs to. Used for personalized content recommendations, targeted advertising, and dynamic user experience customization.

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for preferred audio track language. Used for multi-language content with multiple audio tracks.
Boolean flag indicating whether autoplay of next episode or content is enabled for this profile. True enables automatic playback; false requires manual selection.
Boolean flag indicating whether automatic preview playback is enabled when browsing content. True enables preview autoplay; false disables previews.
URL reference to the profile avatar image stored in the Media Asset Management (MAM) system or Content Delivery Network (CDN). Used for visual identification in profile selection interfaces.

business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Opt-Out Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether the viewer has opted out of personal data sale under CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act). True indicates opt-out; false indicates consent. Required for California viewer data privacy compliance.

business_glossary_term: Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) Compliant Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether this profile is subject to COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act) compliance requirements. True for profiles of children under 13; false for adult profiles. Enforces enhanced privacy protections and parental consent requirements.
Timestamp when the viewer profile was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, lifecycle tracking, and compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Internal Audit Standard; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Compliance

description: Boolean flag indicating whether data saver mode is enabled to reduce bandwidth consumption. True limits video quality and disables high-bandwidth features; false allows full quality streaming.

Boolean flag indicating whether this profile is entitled to download content for offline viewing. True enables download feature; false restricts to streaming only. Controlled by subscription tier and DRM (Digital Rights Management) policies.
reference: DRM (Digital Rights Management) Policy; Zuora Subscription Entitlements

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Timestamp
description: Timestamp when GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) consent was obtained for this viewer profile. Required for EU audience data privacy compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Comma-separated list of preferred content genre identifiers for personalization and recommendation algorithms. Derived from viewing history and explicit user selections. Used by Adobe Experience Platform for segment targeting.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Schema; Content Genre Taxonomy

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default profile that is automatically selected when the subscriber logs in. True for default profile; false for secondary profiles. Only one profile per subscriber should be marked as default.

Boolean flag indicating whether this is a dedicated kids profile with enhanced parental controls, age-appropriate content filtering, and COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act) compliance enforcement. True for kids profiles; false for adult/general profiles.

ISO 639-1 two-letter language code with optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., 'en', 'en-US', 'es-MX'). Determines the language for user interface, content metadata, and audio/subtitle defaults.

Timestamp when the viewer profile was last accessed or used for content viewing. Used for engagement analytics, churn prediction, and inactive profile identification. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Timestamp when the viewer profile was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Internal Audit Standard; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Compliance

description: Platform type of the last device used to access this viewer profile. Used for platform-specific analytics and Over-The-Top (OTT) distribution tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_ios|mobile_android|smart_tv|roku|fire_tv|apple_tv|chromecast|gaming_console — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Device Schema; OTT (Over-The-Top) Platform Taxonomy

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the viewer profile has consented to receive marketing communications and promotional content. True allows marketing; false opts out. Required for GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) compliance.
reference: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 6; CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: Maximum content maturity rating level allowed for this viewer profile. Enforces parental controls and content filtering based on Motion Picture Association (MPA) film ratings and TV Parental Guidelines. Content exceeding this rating is blocked from playback. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: G|PG|PG-13|R|NC-17|TV-Y|TV-Y7|TV-G|TV-PG|TV-14|TV-MA — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Motion Picture Association (MPA) Content Ratings; TV Parental Guidelines

description: Maximum number of concurrent video streams allowed for this viewer profile. Enforces Digital Rights Management (DRM) and subscription tier entitlements. Typical values range from 1 to 4 streams.
reference: DRM (Digital Rights Management) Policy; Zuora Subscription Entitlements

description: Maximum number of content titles that can be downloaded for offline viewing on this profile. Enforces subscription tier limits and storage management policies.
reference: Zuora Subscription Entitlements; DRM (Digital Rights Management) Policy

description: Boolean flag indicating whether personalized content recommendations and user experience customization are enabled for this profile. True enables Adobe Experience Platform personalization; false provides generic content discovery. Impacts GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) consent requirements.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Consent Schema; GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation); CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

business_glossary_term: Profile Personal Identification Number (PIN) Code Hash
description: Hashed PIN code for profile access control and parental lock enforcement. Used to restrict access to mature content or prevent unauthorized profile switching. Stored as cryptographic hash for security.
reference: Internal Security Standard; PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard)

business_glossary_term: Profile Personal Identification Number (PIN) Enabled Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether PIN protection is enabled for this profile. True requires PIN entry for profile access; false allows unrestricted access.
Human-readable display name for the viewer profile (e.g., 'Dad', 'Kids', 'Guest'). Used for profile selection and personalization.
Integer value determining the display order of this profile in the profile selection interface. Lower values appear first. Used for user experience optimization.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform User Interface Schema

description: Current lifecycle state of the viewer profile. Active profiles can access content; inactive profiles are temporarily disabled; suspended profiles are blocked due to policy violations; deleted profiles are soft-deleted and retained for compliance.

Classification of the viewer profile within the household. Primary profiles have full account privileges; secondary profiles may have restricted access; kids profiles enforce parental controls; guest profiles are temporary.
ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for preferred subtitle language. Used when subtitle_preference is enabled.
Default subtitle display preference for this viewer profile. Off disables subtitles; on always displays subtitles; auto displays subtitles based on content language and user language mismatch.
Cumulative total viewing hours for this viewer profile across all content and sessions. Used for engagement metrics, Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) analysis, and Lifetime Value (LTV) calculations.
reference: Nielsen Media Research Audience Measurement; ARPU (Average Revenue Per User); LTV (Lifetime Value)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether viewing history is tracked and stored for this profile. True enables history tracking for recommendations and resume playback; false disables tracking for privacy. Impacts GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) compliance.
reference: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation); CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

The monetary value of the discount applied to the subscription as a result of this redemption. Expressed in the subscription currency.
The marketing source or touchpoint that led to this offer redemption. Examples include email campaign, social media ad, affiliate link, or organic search. Used for marketing attribution analysis.

Net payable amount converted to the company code local currency for general ledger posting and financial reporting.
Net amount payable to vendor after all deductions (discounts, withholding tax), in document currency. This is the amount that will be paid.
Outstanding unpaid amount remaining on this payable, in document currency. Reduced as partial payments are applied. Zero when fully cleared.
Single-character code indicating if payment is blocked (e.g., A=Blocked for Payment, R=Blocked for Review, blank=Not Blocked). Prevents automatic payment until resolved.
Free-text explanation for why payment is blocked (e.g., pending approval, invoice dispute, missing documentation). Null if not blocked.
Single-character code indicating the payment method (e.g., C=Check, T=Wire Transfer, E=Electronic Funds Transfer, A=ACH). Determines payment run processing.
Four-character code defining the payment terms negotiated with the vendor (e.g., net 30, 2/10 net 30). Determines due date and cash discount eligibility.
Date when the accounts payable document was posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period for financial reporting.
Ten-digit purchase order number if this payable originated from a PO-based procurement. Used for three-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice).
Boolean flag indicating whether this payable is subject to SOX financial controls and requires additional approval workflows. True for material transactions.
Total tax amount included in the invoice (VAT, sales tax, GST), in document currency. May include multiple tax jurisdictions.
Free-text description of the payable, typically describing the goods or services invoiced (e.g., 'Q4 2023 content licensing fees', 'Studio equipment rental').
Amount of withholding tax deducted from vendor payment per tax regulations (e.g., talent residuals withholding, foreign vendor withholding), in document currency.
Boolean flag indicating whether withholding tax applies to this payable. True if withholding tax is calculated and deducted.
Unique identifier for the accounts receivable record. Primary key for this entity.
